rules.title: All Scoring statuses are evaluated after the Match ends
rules.description: All Scoring statuses are evaluated after the Match ends
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SC1>{{/BOLD}} All Scoring statuses are evaluated {{BOLD}}after the Match ends{{/BOLD}}. Scores are calculated five (5) seconds after the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} ends, or once all {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} on the Field come to rest, whichever comes first.
  
a. This 5-second delay is intended to be the only permitted “benefit of the doubt” for last-second scoring actions. If an object or {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is still in motion and “too close to call” between two states at the 5-second mark, then the less advantageous of the two states should be awarded to the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}(s) in question. For example:

  
  i. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} which has {{LINK:#parked}}Parked{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} but slowly droops down and is in contact with the top of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} at five (5) seconds would not be considered {{LINK:#parked}}Parked{{/LINK}}.
  ii. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} which slowly falls out of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} at five (5) seconds would not be considered {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}.
b. At the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, the on-screen timer displayed by Tournament Manager will hold the current {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} information and “0:00” for five (5) seconds before moving to queue the next {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. This should be the primary 5-second visual cue used by {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}.
c. This 5-second delay is only intended to be a “benefit of the doubt” grace period, not an extra five (5) seconds of {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} time. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} which are designed to strategically exploit this grace period will receive a {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}}, and any post-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} movement will not be included in score calculation (i.e., the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will be scored as it was at 0:00).
d. Referees should avoid contacting or moving {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} as much as possible while evaluating Scoring statuses. If an object must be moved to evaluate the status of another object, its status must be agreed upon by all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, and noted or recorded, before it is moved.
e. Referees must record counts based on verified scoring statuses evaluated after the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, using final positions of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. Point considerations used to determine whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} is Referees must record counts based on verified scoring statuses evaluated after the{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, using final positions of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. Point considerations used to determine whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} (e.g., specified in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} Notes) should NOT be added to or deducted from the actual score, and points scored during a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} should not be deducted from a score.Match Affecting (e.g., specified in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} Notes) should NOT be added to or deducted from the actual score, and points scored during a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} should not be deducted from a score.

rules.title: A Block is considered Scored if it meets all of the following criteria:
rules.description: A Block is considered Scored if it meets all of the following criteria:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SC2>{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is considered {{BOLD}}Scored{{/BOLD}} if it meets all of the following criteria:
  
a. The {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is in contact with the inside surface(s) of the clear plastic part of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}.
  i. The edge faces of the clear plastic parts are not considered inside surfaces.
b. The {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is not in contact with a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} of the same color as that {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}}.
c. The {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is not in contact with the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:

c. The {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is not in contact with the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: A Control Zone is considered Controlled by an Alliance if a majority of the Blocks Scored in that Control Zone are the same color as the Alliance
rules.description: A Control Zone is considered Controlled by an Alliance if a majority of the Blocks Scored in that Control Zone are the same color as the Alliance
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SC3>{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} is considered {{BOLD}}Controlled{{/BOLD}} by an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} if a majority of the {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in that {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} are the same color as the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
  
a. For Long Goals, a {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is considered {{BOLD}}Scored in the Control Zone{{/BOLD}} if it is entirely contained within that {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}}.
b. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} must be considered {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} (see <SC2>) to also be considered {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} In most cases, if you can’t see a little bit of the next {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} on the “inside” of the tape line (see figure SC3-2), the {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} on that side of the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} probably isn’t entirely contained within the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}}.
 It’s not practical to examine every {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} at a detailed level to decide whether it’s “entirely within” or not. If a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} can’t definitively tell whether a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is entirely contained within a {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} or not, that {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} should be considered {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should not be invited into the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} to contribute opinions; this decision is up to the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: A Robot is considered Parked if it meets all of the following criteria:
rules.description: A Robot is considered Parked if it meets all of the following criteria:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SC4>{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is considered {{BOLD}}Parked{{/BOLD}} if it meets all of the following criteria:
  
a. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not contacting the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} outside of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}.
b. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not contacting any {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} other than the inside face of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} inside of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}, and/or the plastic extrusions and connectors that are part of the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}. Contact with these allowed elements is not required.
c. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is at least partially within the vertical projection of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:

rules.title: Scoring of the Autonomous Bonus is evaluated immediately after the Autonomous Period ends (i
rules.description: Scoring of the Autonomous Bonus is evaluated immediately after the Autonomous Period ends (i
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SC5>{{/BOLD}} Scoring of the {{BOLD}}Autonomous Bonus{{/BOLD}} is evaluated immediately after the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} ends (i.e., once all {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} on the Field come to rest).
  
a. Points for {{LINK:#parked}}Parked{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are not included in the calculation of an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} score for the purposes of determining the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}}.
b. If the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} ends in a tie, including a zero-to-zero tie, each {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will receive an {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} of five (5) points.
c. Any {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}, Major or Minor, committed during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} will result in the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} being automatically awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. See <GG13>.
d. Per rule <GG13>, if both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} commit {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}, then no {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} will be awarded.

rules.title: An Autonomous Win Point is awarded to any Alliance that ends the Autonomous Period with all of the following tasks completed, and that has committed no Violations during the Autonomous Period:
rules.description: An Autonomous Win Point is awarded to any Alliance that ends the Autonomous Period with all of the following tasks completed, and that has committed no Violations during the Autonomous Period:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SC6>{{/BOLD}} An {{BOLD}}Autonomous Win Point{{/BOLD}} is awarded to any {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} that ends the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} with all of the following tasks completed, and that has committed no {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}:
  
a. At least seven (7) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color are {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}.
b. At least three (3) different {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} include at least one (1) {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color.
c. At least three (3) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color have been removed from {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}}.
d. Neither {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is partially or entirely within the vertical projection of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} or contacting the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} barrier. {{BOLD}}For events which qualify directly to the World Championship (e.g., Event Region Championships and Signature Events){{/BOLD}}, the following tasks must be completed for an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} to receive an {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}}. The standard criteria above still apply to all other events.
  
a. At least ten (10) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color are {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}.
b. At least three (3) different {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} include at least two (2) {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color.
c. At least three (3) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color have been removed from {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}}.
d. Neither {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is partially or entirely within the vertical projection of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} or contacting the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} barrier.
 
{{CALLOUT}} {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} criteria may be further modified for the World Championship if needed, with details to be released in a future Game Manual update.
 {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} criteria for World Championship-qualifying events will be used as a baseline to determine criteria for the World Championship. Any potential modifications will be minor, and will provide an increased challenge over the criteria listed above. For example, one possibility could be ”At least twelve (12) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} Scored,” instead of ten (10).
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG4>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Be safe out there
rules.description: Be safe out there
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<S1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Be safe out there.{{/BOLD}} If at any time the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} operation or {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} actions are deemed unsafe or have damaged a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}}, or the Field, the offending {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may receive a {{LINK:#disablement}}Disablement{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} will require re-inspection as described in rule <R3> before it may take the Field again.

 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} should be reported to and/or discussed with the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} during the event, and should be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<S1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Be safe out there.{{/BOLD}} If at any time the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} operation or {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} actions are deemed unsafe or have damaged a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}}, or the Field, the offending {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may receive a {{LINK:#disablement}}Disablement{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} will require re-inspection as described in rule <R3> before it may take the Field again.

 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} should be reported to and/or discussed with the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} during the event, and should be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: Students must be accompanied by an Adult
rules.description: Students must be accompanied by an Adult
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<S2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}{{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} must be accompanied by an Adult.{{/BOLD}} No {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} may attend a V5RC event without a responsible {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} supervising them. The {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} must obey all rules and be careful to not violate {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}-centered policies, but must be present for the full duration of the event in the case of an emergency. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule may result in removal from the event.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} should be reported to the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} during the event, and should be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<S2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}{{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} must be accompanied by an Adult.{{/BOLD}} No {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} may attend a V5RC event without a responsible {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} supervising them. The {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} must obey all rules and be careful to not violate {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}-centered policies, but must be present for the full duration of the event in the case of an emergency. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule may result in removal from the event.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} should be reported to the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} during the event, and should be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: Stay inside the Field
rules.description: Stay inside the Field
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<S3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Stay inside the Field.{{/BOLD}} If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is completely outside of the Field during a match, it will receive a {{LINK:#disablement}}Disablement{{/LINK}} for the remainder of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: The intent of this rule is not to penalize {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} for having mechanisms that inadvertently cross the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} during normal game play.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: Drive Team Members must wear eye protection
rules.description: Drive Team Members must wear eye protection
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<S4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Drive Team Members must wear eye protection.{{/BOLD}} All {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} must wear some form of eye protection while at the Field for {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}. Safety glasses or other eyewear with side shields and non-shattering lenses are recommended. While in the pit and queuing areas, it is highly recommended that all {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members wear eye protection.

Safety glasses or other eyewear with side shields and non-shattering lenses are recommended. While in the pit and queuing areas, it is highly recommended that all {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members wear eye protection.

rules.title: Each Student Team member must have a completed participant release form on file for the event and season
rules.description: Each Student Team member must have a completed participant release form on file for the event and season
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<S5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Each Student Team member must have a completed participant release form on file for the event and season.{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} member cannot participate in an event without a completed release form on file.

rules.title: Treat everyone with respect
rules.description: Treat everyone with respect
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<G1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Treat everyone with respect.{{/BOLD}} All {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are expected to conduct themselves in a respectful and professional manner while competing in VEX V5 Robotics Competition events. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} or any of its members ({{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} or any {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} associated with the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}) are disrespectful or uncivil to event staff, volunteers, or fellow competitors, they may receive a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} from a current or upcoming {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} conduct pertaining to {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} may also impact a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} eligibility for judged awards. Repeated or extreme {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} could result in a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} being {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} from an entire event, depending on the severity of the situation.
 
{{CALLOUT}} We all can contribute to creating a fun and inclusive event experience for all event attendees. Some examples include:
 When dealing with difficult and stressful situations, it is…
  
• Okay for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to be gracious and supportive when your {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} partner makes a mistake.
• Not okay for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to harass, tease, or be disrespectful to your {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} partner when a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} does not go your way. When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} does not understand a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} ruling or score, it is…
  
• Okay for {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} to consult with a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} to discuss a ruling per the process outlined in <T3> in a calm and respectful manner.
• Not okay for {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} to continue arguing with the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} after a decision has been finalized, or for {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} to approach a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} with ruling/scoring concerns. When {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are getting ready for an upcoming {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, it is…
  
• Okay for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} to develop a game strategy that utilizes the strengths of both {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} to cooperatively play the game.
• Not okay for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} to intentionally play beneath their abilities to manipulate the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} results.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
 This rule exists alongside the REC Foundation Code of Conduct. Violation of the Code of Conduct can be considered a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} and can result in {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} from a current {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, an upcoming {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, an entire event, or (in extreme cases) an entire competition season. {{LINK:https://vrc-kb.recf.org/hc/en-us/articles/9653987780375-Code-of-Conduct}}The Code of Conduct can be found here.{{/LINK}}
 More information regarding the event Code of Conduct process {{LINK:https://vrc-kb.recf.org/hc/en-us/articles/16943747839383-Event-Code-of-Conduct-Process}}can be found here.{{/LINK}}
  
a. Event attendees are not allowed to record audio or video of {{LINK:#team}}Teams’{{/LINK}} discussions with {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} or other event staff/volunteers.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: Any {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} may be considered {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} and should be addressed on a case-by-case basis. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} at risk of a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} due to multiple disrespectful or uncivil behaviors will usually receive a “final warning,” although the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} is not required to provide one. All Major {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}/{{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualifications{{/LINK}} should be reported to and/or discussed with the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} during the event, and should be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: V5RC is a student-centered program
rules.description: V5RC is a student-centered program
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<G2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}V5RC is a student-centered program.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} should not make decisions about the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} build, design, or gameplay, and should not provide an unfair advantage by providing ‘help’ that is beyond the {{LINK:#student}}Student’s{{/LINK}} independent abilities. {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} must be prepared to demonstrate an active understanding of their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} design, construction, and programming to judges or event staff. {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} should build, design, and code the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} with minimal {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} involvement.
 
{{CALLOUT}} Some amount of {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} mentorship, teaching, and/or guidance is an expected and encouraged facet of VEX competitions. No one is born an expert in robotics! However, obstacles should always be viewed as teaching opportunities, not problems for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to solve for the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
 When building or designing the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, it is…
  
• Okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to help a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} consider why something failed, so it can be improved.
• Not okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to provide step-by-step instructions or photos for the {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} to copy. When a mechanism falls off, it is…
  
• Okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to help a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} consider why it failed, so it can be improved.
• Not okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to investigate or put the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} back together. When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} encounters a complex programming concept, it is…
  
• Okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to guide a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} through a flowchart to understand its logic.
• Not okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to write a premade command for that {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} to copy/paste. During {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} play, it is…
  
• Okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to provide cheerful, positive encouragement as a spectator.
• Not okay for an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} to explicitly shout step-by-step commands from the audience. {{/CALLOUT}}
 This rule operates in tandem with the {{LINK:https://vrc-kb.recf.org/hc/en-us/articles/9654578622487-Student-Centered-Policy-}}REC Foundation Student Centered Policy{{/LINK}}, which is available in the REC Library for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to reference throughout the season.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: Potential {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule will be reviewed on a case-by-case basis. By definition, all {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule become {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} as soon as a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} which was built or coded by an {{LINK:#adult}}Adult{{/LINK}} wins a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. All reported and/or suspected <G2> {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} should be reported to the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} during the event, and should be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: Use common sense
rules.description: Use common sense
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<G3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Use common sense.{{/BOLD}} When reading and applying the various rules in this document, please remember that common sense always applies in the VEX V5 Robotics Competition.
 
{{CALLOUT}} For example…
  
• If there is an obvious typographical error (such as “per <T5>” instead of “per <GG5>”), this does not mean that the error should be taken literally until corrected in a future update.
• Understand the realities of the VEX V5 Robot construction system. For example, if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} could hover above the Field for a whole {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, that would create loopholes in many of the rules. But... they can’t. So don’t worry about it.
• When in doubt, if there is no rule prohibiting an action, it is generally legal. However, if you have to ask whether a given action would violate <S1>, {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, or <T1>, then that’s probably a good indication that it is outside the spirit of the competition.
• In general, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be given the “benefit of the doubt” in the case of accidental or edge-case rules infractions. However, there is a limit to this allowance, and repeated or strategic infractions will still be penalized.
• This rule also applies to {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules. If a component’s legality cannot be easily/intuitively discerned by the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules as written, then {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should expect additional scrutiny during inspection. This especially applies to those rules which govern non-VEX components (e.g. <R19>, <R20>, <R23>, etc). There is a difference between “creativity” and “lawyering.” Basically, if there’s not a rule that makes a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} part legal, it’s not allowed. {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: All work must represent the skill level of the Students on the Team
rules.description: All work must represent the skill level of the Students on the Team
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}All work must represent the skill level of the Students on the Team.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} design, {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, coding, strategy, and ongoing work must represent the skill level of the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} currently on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must avoid academic dishonesty and should not copy a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or mechanism that has been provided for them. This includes, but is not limited to, the use of instructions, pictures & videos, notebooks, CAD designs, and/or any other documentation useful to the design process provided by anyone that is not a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} (including {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} on another {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}).
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may be inspired by designs by other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}, and use an idea from someone else to spark innovation, but are expected to document and demonstrate this in their engineering notebook alongside evidence of iteration. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are required to present this evidence if asked to do so by a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} inspector, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}, or Judge.
  i. Using elements of another design as a starting point is acceptable if the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is capable of demonstrating evidence of iteration, innovation, and/or modification that makes the design uniquely their own. Documentation should clearly demonstrate the idea that was used for inspiration, and how it was changed for the final implementation on the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. It should be clear that this final implementation is not an exact copy of ANY other original design.
  ii. Failure to demonstrate evidence of iteration, innovation, and/or modification will result in a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} plans and code (e.g., the annual Hero Bot, VEXcode configurations, etc.) provided by VEX Robotics or the RECF, but are encouraged to use these {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, mechanisms, and code only as a starting point that {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} modify, improve, or replace as their skills increase. Plans provided by VEX Robotics or the RECF are the only legal plans available for use in competition.
d. This rule still applies to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} within the same school, organization, or club. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} and/or code sets that are identical or substantially similar to one another across {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} within the same school/organization/club will be considered in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule, regardless of whether they compete in the same or different events. Clause D does not apply in situations where both/all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are using the unmodified Hero Bot design, as allowed by clause C of this rule. For more information, including acceptable and unacceptable examples of mechanical design, construction, coding, and strategy solutions, please refer to the {{LINK:https://v5rc-kb.recf.org/hc/en-us/articles/9654578622487-Student-Centered-Policy#student-centered-examples-HFfvG}}Student Centered Policy{{/LINK}} in the RECF Library.
 
{{CALLOUT}} The VEX Robotics Competition and the Robotics Education & Competition Foundation (RECF) recognize that many third-party individuals and organizations produce and distribute {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} designs, instructions, and/or other materials that are not under our direct control. We cannot legally regulate or restrict the activities of these external entities. However, when {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} use these resources in ways that violate the spirit and letter of the VEX Robotics Game Manual—particularly Rules <G2> and <G4>—they undermine the core mission of the program: to provide {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} with hands-on opportunities to learn, design, and innovate.
 While it is never our intent to punish {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}, we can legally regulate and restrict the activities of the {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in our competitions, and we must preserve the fairness, educational value, and integrity of the competition. Therefore, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} found to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of these rules will be held accountable to the strictest interpretations of <G2> and <G4>.
 It is the responsibility of each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to be able to explain and defend the design, construction, and programming of their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} if questioned by referees, inspectors, {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}}, or judges. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should be prepared to describe their design process, justify design decisions, and demonstrate a full understanding of how their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and code function.
 {{BOLD}}If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is unable, for any reason, to provide reasonable evidence (when requested by event staff) that their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and code are the result of their own work, it is appropriate to assume that the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <G2> and/or <G4>.
{{/BOLD}} Event organizers cannot reasonably know the origins of every design or independently verify whether a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} was created from scratch, purchased online, or copied from pictures of another {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} design. When questions of authenticity arise, the only direct and fair approach is to require {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to explain and defend their work. This is not unlike academic honesty concerns in schools, and intellectual property concerns in business. By requiring {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to defend their designs, we ensure that they are developing not only technical skills, but effective communication skills and accountability, as well.
 Consequences may include disqualification from matches, removal from events, and/or escalation of the investigation to VEX Robotics and the RECF for further disciplinary action, which may include sanctions up to and including removal from the program.
 Event staff should bear in mind <G3>, and use common sense when enforcing this rule. It is not the intent to actively hunt for {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule, compare every {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at an event to all other known {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} designs, or closely question every team at an event about their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot's{{/LINK}} code. This rule is a set of tools for use if potential {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} are noted by or reported to event staff, and it is expected that most {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will never be required to defend their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} design or code.
 {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} or individuals who deliberately weaponize, manipulate, or falsely report <G4> {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} for competitive gain or to harass another {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may be subject to a separate RECF Code of Conduct investigation. Misuse of this rule is considered a serious {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

  Significant Q&As:
  
  • {{LINK:https://www.robotevents.com/V5RC/2024-2025/QA/2677}}2677{{/LINK}} - Teams may only use custom parts that were designed and created by the Students on that Team
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
  • {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} believed to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule should be reported to the Judge Advisor, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} for further investigation in coordination with the RSM. Based on the investigation the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may be removed from further {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}, have their Robot Skills Challenge scores removed, and/or be removed from consideration from judged awards.
  • {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis, in tandem with the RECF Student Centered Policy as noted in <G2>, and the REC Foundation Code of Conduct as noted in {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}. All reported and/or suspected <G4> {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} should be reported to the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} during the event, and should be reported to the RECF Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}All work must represent the skill level of the Students on the Team.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} design, {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, coding, strategy, and ongoing work must represent the skill level of the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} currently on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must avoid academic dishonesty and should not copy a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or mechanism that has been provided for them. This includes, but is not limited to, the use of instructions, pictures & videos, notebooks, CAD designs, and/or any other documentation useful to the design process provided by anyone that is not a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} (including {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} on another {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}).
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may be inspired by designs by other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}, and use an idea from someone else to spark innovation, but are expected to document and demonstrate this in their engineering notebook alongside evidence of iteration. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are required to present this evidence if asked to do so by a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} inspector, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}, or Judge.
  i. Using elements of another design as a starting point is acceptable if the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is capable of demonstrating evidence of iteration, innovation, and/or modification that makes the design uniquely their own. Documentation should clearly demonstrate the idea that was used for inspiration, and how it was changed for the final implementation on the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. It should be clear that this final implementation is not an exact copy of ANY other original design.
  ii. Failure to demonstrate evidence of iteration, innovation, and/or modification will result in a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} plans and code (e.g., the annual Hero Bot, VEXcode configurations, etc.) provided by VEX Robotics or the RECF, but are encouraged to use these {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, mechanisms, and code only as a starting point that {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} modify, improve, or replace as their skills increase. Plans provided by VEX Robotics or the RECF are the only legal plans available for use in competition.
d. This rule still applies to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} within the same school, organization, or club. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} and/or code sets that are identical or substantially similar to one another across {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} within the same school/organization/club will be considered in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule, regardless of whether they compete in the same or different events. Clause D does not apply in situations where both/all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are using the unmodified Hero Bot design, as allowed by clause C of this rule. For more information, including acceptable and unacceptable examples of mechanical design, construction, coding, and strategy solutions, please refer to the {{LINK:https://v5rc-kb.recf.org/hc/en-us/articles/9654578622487-Student-Centered-Policy#student-centered-examples-HFfvG}}Student Centered Policy{{/LINK}} in the RECF Library.
 
{{CALLOUT}} The VEX Robotics Competition and the Robotics Education & Competition Foundation (RECF) recognize that many third-party individuals and organizations produce and distribute {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} designs, instructions, and/or other materials that are not under our direct control. We cannot legally regulate or restrict the activities of these external entities. However, when {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} use these resources in ways that violate the spirit and letter of the VEX Robotics Game Manual—particularly Rules <G2> and <G4>—they undermine the core mission of the program: to provide {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} with hands-on opportunities to learn, design, and innovate.
 While it is never our intent to punish {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}, we can legally regulate and restrict the activities of the {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in our competitions, and we must preserve the fairness, educational value, and integrity of the competition. Therefore, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} found to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of these rules will be held accountable to the strictest interpretations of <G2> and <G4>.
 It is the responsibility of each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to be able to explain and defend the design, construction, and programming of their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} if questioned by referees, inspectors, {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}}, or judges. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should be prepared to describe their design process, justify design decisions, and demonstrate a full understanding of how their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and code function.
 {{BOLD}}If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is unable, for any reason, to provide reasonable evidence (when requested by event staff) that their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and code are the result of their own work, it is appropriate to assume that the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <G2> and/or <G4>.
{{/BOLD}} Event organizers cannot reasonably know the origins of every design or independently verify whether a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} was created from scratch, purchased online, or copied from pictures of another {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} design. When questions of authenticity arise, the only direct and fair approach is to require {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to explain and defend their work. This is not unlike academic honesty concerns in schools, and intellectual property concerns in business. By requiring {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to defend their designs, we ensure that they are developing not only technical skills, but effective communication skills and accountability, as well.
 Consequences may include disqualification from matches, removal from events, and/or escalation of the investigation to VEX Robotics and the RECF for further disciplinary action, which may include sanctions up to and including removal from the program.
 Event staff should bear in mind <G3>, and use common sense when enforcing this rule. It is not the intent to actively hunt for {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule, compare every {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at an event to all other known {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} designs, or closely question every team at an event about their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot's{{/LINK}} code. This rule is a set of tools for use if potential {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} are noted by or reported to event staff, and it is expected that most {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will never be required to defend their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} design or code.

Misuse of this rule is considered a serious {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.

{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
  • {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} believed to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule should be reported to the Judge Advisor, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} for further investigation in coordination with the RSM. rules.title: Each Student can only belong to one Team
rules.description: Each Student can only belong to one Team
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<G5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Each Student can only belong to one Team.{{/BOLD}} Each Team must include {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}}(s), {{LINK:#team}}Designer{{/LINK}}(s), and {{LINK:#team}}Builder{{/LINK}}(s). Many also include notebooker(s). No {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} may fulfill any of these roles for more than one VEX V5 Robotics Competition {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} in a given competition season. {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} may have more than one role on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, e.g., the {{LINK:#team}}Designer{{/LINK}} may also be the {{LINK:#team}}Builder{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}}, and a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members may only move from one {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to another for non-strategic reasons outside of the {{LINK:#team}}Team's{{/LINK}} control.
  i. Examples of permissible moves may include, but are not limited to, changing schools, conflicts within a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, or combining/splitting {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}.
  ii. Examples of strategic moves in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule may include, but are not limited to, one {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}} “switching” {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in order to program multiple {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, one {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} designing multiple teams’ {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, or one {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} writing the Engineering Notebook for multiple {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}.
  iii. If a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} leaves a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to join another {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, <G4> still applies to the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} remaining on the previous {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. For example, if a {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}} leaves a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, then that {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must still represent the skill level of the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} without that {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}}. One way to accomplish this would be to ensure that the {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}} teaches or trains a “replacement” {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}} in their absence.
  iv. Points ii and iii are intended to represent real-world situations that are found in industry engineering. If a vital member of a professional engineering team were to suddenly leave, the remaining members of the team should still be capable of working on / maintaining their project.
b. When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} qualifies for a Championship event (e.g., States, Nationals, Worlds, etc.) the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} attending the Championship event are expected to be the same {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that was awarded the spot. {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} can be added as support to the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, but may not be added as {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#team}}Coders{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
  i. An exception is allowed if only one (1) member of the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is able to attend the event. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} can make a single substitution of a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}} for the Championship event with another {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}, even if that {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} has competed on a different {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. This {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} will now be a member of this new {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} and may not substitute back to the original {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} during the season. Note: {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} cannot “borrow” {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} from other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to serve as {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Coders{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Designers{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#team}}Builders{{/LINK}} or notebookers. However, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} can add permanent members throughout the season under the guidelines of this rule.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} believed to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule should be reported to the Judge Advisor, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} for further investigation in coordination with the RSM. {{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
{{CALLOUT}} {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} should bear in mind <G3>, and use common sense when enforcing this rule. It is not the intent to punish a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who may change {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members over the course of a season due to illness, changing schools, conflicts within a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, etc.
 {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} and referees are not expected to keep a roster of any {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} who has ever been a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} for one day. This rule is intended to block any instance of loaning or sharing {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members for the sole purpose of gaining a competitive advantage.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Only Drive Team Members, and only in the Alliance Station
rules.description: Only Drive Team Members, and only in the Alliance Station
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Only Drive Team Members, and only in the Alliance Station.{{/BOLD}} During a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may be operated only by the {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} and/or by software running on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} control system in accordance with <R11> and <GG11>. A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may send up to three (3) {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} to their {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} for each {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, and those {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} must remain in their {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} for the duration of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
 {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are the only {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members that are allowed to be in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} (other than event staff) are not permitted to be in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
 {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are prohibited from any of the following actions during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}:
  
a. {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are prohibited from any of the following actions during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}:
  a. Using any sort of communication device in the {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}}. Non-headphone devices with communication features turned off (e.g. a phone in airplane mode or a walkie talkie turned off) are allowed. If communication features are needed for translation apps during post-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} discussions, it should not be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
  b. Standing or sitting on any sort of object during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, regardless of whether the Field is on the floor or elevated, except as required by an official {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/22353712963095-Needs-based-Special-Accommodations}}accommodation request{{/LINK}} that has been approved by the REC Foundation.
  c. Bringing/using additional materials to simplify the game challenge during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  d. To ensure that {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are aware of verbal calls during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (as an application of rules <T1>, {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, <S1>, and <G3>), powered headphones, earbuds, and/or passive earpieces connected to electronic devices cannot be worn/used in the {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} except as required by an official {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/22353712963095-Needs-based-Special-Accommodations}}accommodation request{{/LINK}} that has been approved by the REC Foundation.
ii. Individuals who are not {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} for a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} cannot provide directions, commands, or advice to the {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} during that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. They're welcome to provide cheerful, positive encouragement, but should not affect {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} play or strategy.
{{CALLOUT}} Point iii is intended to refer to non-{{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}-related items that directly influence gameplay, such as a speaker that plays a buzzer sound to distract your opponent. Provided no other rules are violated, and the items do not pose any safety or Field damage risks, the following examples are not considered {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of <GG1>:

  
• Materials used before or after a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, such as a pre-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} alignment aid
• Strategic aids, such as a whiteboard or clipboard
• Earplugs, gloves, or other personal accessories {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule are not required to be {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}, and could invoke {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of other rules, such as {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, <G2>, or <G4>.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

a. {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are prohibited from any of the following actions during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}:
  a. Using any sort of communication device in the {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}}. Non-headphone devices with communication features turned off (e.g. a phone in airplane mode or a walkie talkie turned off) are allowed. If communication features are needed for translation apps during post-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} discussions, it should not be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
  b. Standing or sitting on any sort of object during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, regardless of whether the Field is on the floor or elevated, except as required by an official {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/22353712963095-Needs-based-Special-Accommodations}}accommodation request{{/LINK}} that has been approved by the REC Foundation.
  c. Bringing/using additional materials to simplify the game challenge during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  d. To ensure that {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are aware of verbal calls during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (as an application of rules <T1>, {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, <S1>, and <G3>), powered headphones, earbuds, and/or passive earpieces connected to electronic devices cannot be worn/used in the {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} except as required by an official {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/22353712963095-Needs-based-Special-Accommodations}}accommodation request{{/LINK}} that has been approved by the REC Foundation.
ii. Individuals who are not {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} for a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} cannot provide directions, commands, or advice to the {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} during that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. They're welcome to provide cheerful, positive encouragement, but should not affect {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} play or strategy.
{{CALLOUT}} Point iii is intended to refer to non-{{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}-related items that directly influence gameplay, such as a speaker that plays a buzzer sound to distract your opponent. Provided no other rules are violated, and the items do not pose any safety or Field damage risks, the following examples are not considered {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of <GG1>:

  
• Materials used before or after a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, such as a pre-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} alignment aid
• Strategic aids, such as a whiteboard or clipboard
• Earplugs, gloves, or other personal accessories {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule are not required to be {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}, and could invoke {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of other rules, such as {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, <G2>, or <G4>.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: A Team’s Robot should attend every Match
rules.description: A Team’s Robot should attend every Match
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}A Team’s Robot should attend every Match.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must report to the Field for the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} assigned {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, even if the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not functional. If the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not at the Field for the entire duration of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will be considered a “no-show” and receive zero (0) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Points{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are expected to participate in all scheduled {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}(if they’re an {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}} or were selected to join an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} for {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}). Failure to attend scheduled {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} may be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} and the Code of Conduct. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that participate in zero {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} cannot be considered for Judged Awards.

rules.title: Robots on the Field must be ready to play
rules.description: Robots on the Field must be ready to play
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots on the Field must be ready to play.{{/BOLD}} When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} puts their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on the Field, it must be prepared to play (e.g., batteries charged, sized within the starting size constraint, includes only the correct {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-color license plates, etc.).
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who use VEX pneumatics must have their systems charged before they place the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on the Field.
b. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must be placed on the Field promptly. Repeated failure to do so could result in a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}. The exact definition of the term “promptly” is at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}, who will consider event schedule, previous {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} or delays, etc.
c. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is delaying the scheduled start of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, it may be removed from the Field at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may remain at the Field so that the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} does not get assessed a “no-show” (per <GG2>).
d. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not placed on the Field prior to the start of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, it cannot be placed on the Field during that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
e. If an event is using Smart Field Control and a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is unable to successfully connect to Smart Field Control prior to the scheduled start of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} may ask the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} to remove their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} from the Field in accordance with clause C.
  i. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that connects to Smart Field Control but displays a ‘Legacy Field Control’ error on the field monitor is NOT considered successfully connected to Smart Field Control, and may be removed from the Field if it is delaying the scheduled start of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Hands out of the Field
rules.description: Hands out of the Field
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Hands out of the Field.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are prohibited from making intentional contact with any {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, apart from the contact specified in <GG4a> or while introducing {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Loads{{/LINK}} as described in rule {{LINK:#_idTextAnchor086}}<SG9>{{/LINK}}.
  
a. During the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} may only touch their own {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} if the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has not moved at all during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. Touching the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} in this case is permitted only for the following reasons:

  
  i. Turning the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on or off.
  ii. Plugging in a battery.
  iii. Plugging in a V5 Robot Radio.
  iv. Touching the V5 Robot Brain screen, such as to start a program.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: Movement caused by an external force, such as another {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, should not prevent a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} from interacting with their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} under this rule.
{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
a. {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are not permitted to reach into the 3-dimensional volume of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} at any time during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, apart from the actions described above. Rule <S1> applies.
b. Transitive contact, such as contact with the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} that causes the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} to contact {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} inside of the Field, could be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule.
c. Any concerns regarding {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} starting positions should be raised with the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} prior to the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members may never adjust {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} themselves.
{{CALLOUT}} If a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member's{{/LINK}} hands extend over the Field and/or {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} in a way that is safe and doesn’t contact anything in the Field, it’s unlikely to be a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. However, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} may still ask {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} to step back and remain completely outside the field when necessary (e.g., for safety reasons or to reduce the chances of gameplay interference).
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Match replays are allowed, but rare
rules.description: Match replays are allowed, but rare
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Match replays are allowed, but rare.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} replays (i.e., playing a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} over again from its start) must be agreed upon by both the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, and will only be issued in the most extreme circumstances. Some example situations that may warrant a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} replay are as follows (note that this is not an exhaustive list):
  
a. {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} “Field fault” issues.

  
  i. {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} starting in incorrect positions, and out of the allowed tolerances (see <T5>).
  ii. Tape lines lifting.
  iii. {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} detaching or moving beyond normal tolerances (not as a result of {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} interactions).
  iv. The {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} ending early.
  v. Field control disconnecting or {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabling{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. Note, this is sometimes confused with a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} whose motors have overheated, or bent pins on a controller’s competition port causing intermittent drop-outs. In general, any true Field fault will impact both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} simultaneously, not one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at a time.
b. {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} game rule issues.

  
  i. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#disablement}}Disables{{/LINK}} a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for a misinterpretation of a rule {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
  ii. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} starts the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} without determining the outcome of the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} winner.
  iii. The Field is reset before a score is determined.
  iv. A {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is run before its scheduled time without a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} Note: As of the 2024-2025 season, the V5 white screen error is no longer a permitted cause for a guaranteed replay. {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/18341400395156-Troubleshooting-the-V5-Brain-White-Screen-Error}}More information about this error can be found here.{{/LINK}}
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Disqualifications
rules.description: Disqualifications
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Disqualifications.{{/BOLD}} When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} receives a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}, they receive a score of zero (0) for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, as well as zero (0) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Points{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}}.
  
a. If the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} receiving the {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} is on the winning {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}, then {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} on the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} who are not also {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} will receive the win for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and two (2) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. The {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} non-{{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} Partner is unaffected, i.e., they will also receive the win for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and two (2) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}.
b. If the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} was a tie, then each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} on the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} (the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} that did not receive the {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}) will receive the win for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and two (2) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}. If both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} have a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} receiving a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, then all non-{{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will receive a tie for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and one (1) Win Point.
c. {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Points{{/LINK}} are not given to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that receive a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, and are not automatically awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
  When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} in an {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Match{{/LINK}}, the entire {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}}; they receive a loss for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} is awarded the win. If both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} receive a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} in an {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Match{{/LINK}}, both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} receive a loss and will play another {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} to determine a winner.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}, a score of zero (0) will be recorded for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

c. {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Points{{/LINK}} are not given to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that receive a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, and are not automatically awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
  When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} in an {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Match{{/LINK}}, the entire {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}}; they receive a loss for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} is awarded the win. If both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} receive a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} in an {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Match{{/LINK}}, both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} receive a loss and will play another {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} to determine a winner.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}, a score of zero (0) will be recorded for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: Time Outs
rules.description: Time Outs
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Time Outs.{{/BOLD}} Each Elimination {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} gets one three-minute Time Out, which they may request during the {{LINK:#eliminationbracket}}Elimination Bracket{{/LINK}}. The Time Out will be served at the time of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} next upcoming {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} must request their Time Out between {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}; they may not use their Time Out during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, for another {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, or after they have been eliminated. There are no Time Outs during the {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} schedule.
  
a. A Time Out can be ended early, but only if agreed to by both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}.
b. An {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance's{{/LINK}} Time Out request should never be denied if the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} legitimately needs extra time.

rules.title: Keep your Robots together
rules.description: Keep your Robots together

rules.title: Don't hook your Robot to the Field, and don't get Entangled
rules.description: Don't hook your Robot to the Field, and don't get Entangled
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG9>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Don't hook your Robot to the Field, and don't get Entangled.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may not intentionally grasp, grapple, hook, attach to or otherwise {{LINK:#entangle}}Entangle{{/LINK}} with any {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}. Strategies with mechanisms that react against multiple sides of a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} in an effort to latch or hook onto said {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} are prohibited. The intent of this rule is to prevent {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} from unintentionally damaging the Field and/or from anchoring to or otherwise {{LINK:#entangle}}Entangling{{/LINK}} themselves with the Field.
 Whenever possible, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} should alert {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to potential {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} before they happen to prevent actual {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} takes immediate action to avoid or resolve the issue, and if the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} determines that the issue had no effect on the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, no {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} should be recorded.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is reaching inside the open top portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}, it is that {{LINK:#team}}Team's{{/LINK}} responsibility to ensure that the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} doesn't become {{LINK:#entangle}}Entangled{{/LINK}} with the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} when approached by other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: The red Alliance places last
rules.description: The red Alliance places last

rules.title: Controllers must stay connected to the Field
rules.description: Controllers must stay connected to the Field
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG11>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Controllers must stay connected to the Field.{{/BOLD}} Prior to the beginning of each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} must plug their V5 Controller into the Field’s control system. This cable must remain plugged in for the duration of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and may not be removed until the “all-clear” has been given for {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} to retrieve their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. See <T8> for more information regarding Field control system options.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: The intent of this rule is to ensure that {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} abide by commands sent by the tournament software. Temporarily removing the cable to assist with mid-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} troubleshooting, with an {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} or other event technical staff present and assisting, would not be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: Autonomous means “no humans
rules.description: Autonomous means “no humans
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG12>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Autonomous means “no humans.”{{/BOLD}} During the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are not permitted to interact with the {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} in any way, directly or indirectly. This could include, but is not limited to:
  
• Activating any controls on their V5 Controllers
• Unplugging or otherwise manually interfering with the Field connection in any way
• Manually triggering sensors (including the Vision Sensor) in any way, even without touching them Note: In extreme cases, with permission from the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may {{LINK:#disablement}}Disable{{/LINK}} their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} by holding the power button on their V5 Controller. This exception is only intended for egregious safety- or damage-related circumstances; {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabling{{/LINK}} an autonomous routine for strategic purposes would still be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <GG12>.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: See <GG13>.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
