This exception is only intended for egregious safety- or damage-related circumstances; {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabling{{/LINK}} an autonomous routine for strategic purposes would still be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <GG12>.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: See <GG13>.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: All rules still apply in the Autonomous Period
rules.description: All rules still apply in the Autonomous Period

rules.title: Don’t destroy other Robots
rules.description: Don’t destroy other Robots
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG14>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Don’t destroy other Robots. But, be prepared to encounter defense.{{/BOLD}} Strategies aimed solely at the destruction, damage, tipping over, or {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}} of opposing {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are not part of the ethos of the VEX V5 Robotics Competition and are not allowed.
  
a. V5RC Push Back is intended to be an {{LINK:#offensive}}Offensive{{/LINK}} game. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that partake in solely {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} or destructive strategies will not have the protections implied by this rule (see <GG15>). However, {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} play which does not involve destructive or illegal strategies is still within the spirit of this rule.
b. V5RC Push Back is also intended to be an interactive game. Some incidental tipping, {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}}, and damage may occur as a part of normal gameplay without {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. It will be up to the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion whether the interaction was incidental or intentional.
c. A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is responsible for the actions of its {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at all times, including the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. This applies both to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that are driving recklessly or potentially causing damage, and to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that drive around with a small wheel base. A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} should design its {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} such that it is not easily tipped over or damaged by minor contact.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule are not required to be {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}. Intentional and/or egregious tipping, {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}}, or damage may be considered a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion.
• Repeated {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} within a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} or tournament could be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} and/or <S1> at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion.

rules.title: Offensive Robots get the “benefit of the doubt” when judgement calls are required
rules.description: Offensive Robots get the “benefit of the doubt” when judgement calls are required

rules.title: You can’t force an opponent into a penalty
rules.description: You can’t force an opponent into a penalty
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG16>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}You can’t force an opponent into a penalty.{{/BOLD}} Intentional strategies that cause an opponent to break a rule are not permitted, and will not result in a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} for the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: In most cases, if a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} causes their opponent to break a rule, the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} will simply not enforce the penalty on that opponent, and it will be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that was forced into a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. However, if the forced situation becomes {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} in favor of the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that forced the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}, it will be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that forced the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<GG16>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}You can’t force an opponent into a penalty.{{/BOLD}} Intentional strategies that cause an opponent to break a rule are not permitted, and will not result in a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} for the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: In most cases, if a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} causes their opponent to break a rule, the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} will simply not enforce the penalty on that opponent, and it will be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that was forced into a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. However, if the forced situation becomes {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} in favor of the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that forced the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}, it will be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that forced the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: No Holding for more than a 3-count
rules.description: No Holding for more than a 3-count
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<GG17>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}No Holding for more than a 3-count.{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may not {{LINK:#hold}}Hold{{/LINK}} an opposing {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for more than a 3-count during the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}.
 For the purposes of this rule, a “count” is defined as an interval of time that is approximately one second in duration, and “counted out” by {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} verbally. A {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} count should begin immediately once the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} observes a suspected {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} interaction.
 A {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} count should pause when at least one of the following conditions is met:
  
a. The two {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are separated by at least two (2) feet (approximately one foam tile).
b. Either {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has moved at least two (2) feet away (approximately one tile) from the location where the {{LINK:#trap}}Trapping{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#pin}}Pinning{{/LINK}} count began.

  
  i. In the case of {{LINK:#lift}}Lifting{{/LINK}}, this location is measured from where the {{LINK:#lift}}Lifted{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is released, not from where the {{LINK:#lift}}Lifting{{/LINK}} began.
c. The {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} becomes {{LINK:#trap}}Trapped{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#pin}}Pinned{{/LINK}} by a different {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. In this case, the original count would end, and a new count would begin for the newly {{LINK:#trap}}Trapped{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#pin}}Pinned{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
d. In the case of {{LINK:#trap}}Trapping{{/LINK}}, if an avenue of escape becomes available due to changing circumstances in the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  After a {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} count ends, a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may not resume {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} the same {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} again for a 5-count. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} resumes {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} the same {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} within that 5-count, the original {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} count will resume from where it ended. A {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} should use fingers to display the 5-count that occurs after the end of a {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} count, and “wave it off” after the {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} interaction has been cleared.
 
{{CALLOUT}} If the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} determines that a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not attempting to escape, then it is not considered {{LINK:#pin}}Pinned{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#trap}}Trapped{{/LINK}}. This commonly occurs when the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has malfunctioned and lost the ability to move, or when the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is defending a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}.
 This criteria is not required for {{LINK:#lift}}Lifting{{/LINK}}; the {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} status begins as soon as the opponent becomes {{LINK:#lift}}Lifted{{/LINK}}.
 {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} is a standard and legal part of Head-to-Head game play, and only becomes a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} if it exceeds the guidelines in this rule. By beginning a {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} count immediately after noticing a {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} interaction, and providing a visual signal when a {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} interaction has been cleared, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} can help {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} avoid penalties.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Use Blocks to play the game
rules.description: Use Blocks to play the game

rules.title: Starting a Match
rules.description: Starting a Match
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Starting a Match.{{/BOLD}} Prior to the start of each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be placed such that it meets all of the following criteria:
  
a. No larger than 18” (457.2 mm) long by 18” (457.2 mm) wide by 18” (457.2 mm) tall.
b. Not contacting any {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} other than a maximum of one (1) Preload. See rule <SG5>.
c. Not contacting any {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}}.
d. Not contacting any other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
e. Completely stationary (i.e., no motors or other mechanisms in motion).
f. Contacting and/or at least partially within the vertical projection of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: Using external influences, such as Preloads or the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, to maintain a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} starting size is only acceptable if the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} would still satisfy the constraints of <R5> and pass inspection without these influences.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: The {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will not begin with any conditions in this rule unmet. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} cannot meet these conditions in a timely manner, the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} will be removed from the Field and rules <R3d> and <GG2> will apply until the situation is corrected. They will not receive a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, but they will not be permitted to play in the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG7>
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<SG1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Starting a Match.{{/BOLD}} Prior to the start of each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be placed such that it meets all of the following criteria:
  
a. No larger than 18” (457.2 mm) long by 18” (457.2 mm) wide by 18” (457.2 mm) tall.
b. Not contacting any {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} other than a maximum of one (1) Preload. See rule <SG5>.
c. Not contacting any {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}}.
d. Not contacting any other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
e. Completely stationary (i.e., no motors or other mechanisms in motion).
f. Contacting and/or at least partially within the vertical projection of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: Using external influences, such as Preloads or the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, to maintain a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} starting size is only acceptable if the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} would still satisfy the constraints of <R5> and pass inspection without these influences.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: The {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will not begin with any conditions in this rule unmet. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} cannot meet these conditions in a timely manner, the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} will be removed from the Field and rules <R3d> and <GG2> will apply until the situation is corrected. They will not receive a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, but they will not be permitted to play in the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG7>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Horizontal expansion is limited
rules.description: Horizontal expansion is limited
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Horizontal expansion is limited.{{/BOLD}} Once the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may expand horizontally beyond the 18” x 18” starting size limit within the following criteria:
  
a. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can never be larger than 22” wide or 22” long (must always be able to fit within a 22”x22” square horizontal footprint).
  
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• The primary intent of this rule is to limit {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} horizontal expansion. As such, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} that expand horizontally in the vicinity of multiple {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} may be subject to rule <GG15>, and will not receive the “benefit of the doubt” in the case of any {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} judgment calls.
• Incidental/insignificant infractions that occur during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} are only considered {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}}. Repeated {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} should only escalate to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} in extreme circumstances. Examples of {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} include, but are not limited to:
  • Loose wires
  • Broken zip ties / rubber bands
  • Bent or broken mechanical components that are not used for strategic gain
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG2>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Vertical expansion is limited
rules.description: Vertical expansion is limited
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Vertical expansion is limited.{{/BOLD}} Once the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may expand vertically beyond the 18” starting size limit within the following criteria:
  
a. No part of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may exceed an overall height of 22” at any point during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (must always be able to fit within a hypothetical 22”x22”x22” cubic sizing box).
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG2>
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<SG3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Vertical expansion is limited.{{/BOLD}} Once the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may expand vertically beyond the 18” starting size limit within the following criteria:
  
a. No part of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may exceed an overall height of 22” at any point during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (must always be able to fit within a hypothetical 22”x22”x22” cubic sizing box).
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG2>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Keep Blocks in the Field
rules.description: Keep Blocks in the Field
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Keep Blocks in the Field.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not remove {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from the Field. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} that leaves the field during {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} play, will be given to a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} from the same color {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} as the {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} and may be used as a {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} in accordance with <SG9>.

  
a. If a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is leaving the Field (as determined by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}), but is deflected back into the field by a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}}, field monitor, ceiling/wall, or other external factor, it should still be considered “out of the field” and removed by a scorekeeper or {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. If the redirection occurred due to contact with a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}}, it will be at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion whether or not <GG4> (hands out of the field) should apply.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• After a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} third {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} with any {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this rule (either Major or Minor), all subsequent {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule will immediately escalate to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}.
• Any {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that removes three (3) or more {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from the Field in a single {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will receive a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}.
• If it is not clear which {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} was the last to contact the {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}}, all involved {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} with a color that is opposite to the {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} will receive a Violation.
• Due to the difficulty of determining {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} implications of this rule, most {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} should be considered Minor. However, blatantly intentional and/or {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} (especially during {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}) may still immediately escalate to a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion.

rules.title: Each Robot gets one Block as a Preload
rules.description: Each Robot gets one Block as a Preload

rules.title: A Robot may carry, push, or plow an unlimited number of Blocks
rules.description: A Robot may carry, push, or plow an unlimited number of Blocks
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<SG6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}A Robot may carry, push, or plow an unlimited number of Blocks.{{/BOLD}} However, horizontal and vertical expansion limits apply to all parts of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for the entire duration of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. See rules <SG2> and <SG3>.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<SG6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}A Robot may carry, push, or plow an unlimited number of Blocks.{{/BOLD}} However, horizontal and vertical expansion limits apply to all parts of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for the entire duration of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. See rules <SG2> and <SG3>.

rules.title: Don’t cross the Autonomous Line, and don’t interfere with your opponents’ actions
rules.description: Don’t cross the Autonomous Line, and don’t interfere with your opponents’ actions
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Don’t cross the Autonomous Line, and don’t interfere with your opponents’ actions.{{/BOLD}} During the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may not contact foam tiles, {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} which are on the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} side of the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}.
 
a. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in contact with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} are not considered to be on either side, and may be utilized by either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. For the purpose of this rule, all eight {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in groups that cross the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} are considered to be in contact with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}. See Figure SG-7.
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may interact with the portions of each {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} that are on their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} side of the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}, but may not reach across to interact with the “open” portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} on their opponents’ side of the Field. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that engages with an open portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} in proximity to the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} is considered to be interacting with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}, and <SG8> applies to any resulting interactions.
c. Direct contact with any of the following during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} will result in the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} and an {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} being awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}, unless they are ineligible through <SC5> clause C:

  
  i. An opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that isn’t interacting with either the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} or objects that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} positioned above or in contact with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}.
  ii. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} on the other side of the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}.
d. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} should only cross the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} through {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this clause that affect the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} autonomous routines will result in the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} being awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} unless they’re ineligible through clause C. Examples of affecting the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} could include, but are not limited to, a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} moving another {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} out of its starting position so the opponent misses it or a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} crossing the Field and being picked up by an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. This clause does not include the {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} described in clause A.
e. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that employ deliberate {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} autonomous strategies that impact their opponents’ autonomous routines in ways not specifically prohibited by previous clauses, such as intentionally placing {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} on the other side of the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}, may be subject to {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. For example, causing a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} to contact a stationary {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} if the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} determines it was a deliberate {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} action.
{{CALLOUT}}The {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} should be primarily {{LINK:#offensive}}Offensive{{/LINK}}, with {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} focusing on scoring and executing strategic maneuvers rather than {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} disruption. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should avoid actions that are primarily {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} in nature and try to prevent impacts to the opponent’s side of the Field.{{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{IMAGE:https://content.vexrobotics.com/docs/25-26/v5rc-push-back/online-manual/images/SG7.png}}
 {{BOLD}}Figure SG-7:{{/BOLD}} These {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} (circled in red) would be considered to be in contact with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• All {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule (Major or Minor) will result in the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} being awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}; some also award an {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}}. See <SG8> for a potential exception caused by {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} interactions.
• Intentional, strategic, or egregious {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}, such as intentional or repeated contact with an opposing {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} while contacting the foam tiles on the opposing side of the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}, will be considered {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} and should result in a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
• Deliberate {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} autonomous strategies, as described in clause E, may also be recorded as {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion.

For example, causing a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} to contact a stationary {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} if the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} determines it was a deliberate {{LINK:#defensive}}Defensive{{/LINK}} action.

{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• All {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule (Major or Minor) will result in the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} being awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}; some also award an {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}}. See <SG8> for a potential exception caused by {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} interactions.

rules.title: Engage with the Autonomous Line at your own risk
rules.description: Engage with the Autonomous Line at your own risk
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Engage with the Autonomous Line at your own risk.{{/BOLD}} Any {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} who engages with {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} and/or engages with an open portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} in proximity to the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} should be aware that opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may also choose to do the same. Per <GG12> and <GG13>, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are responsible for the actions of their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} at all times.
 During the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}, when {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} from opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} are both engaged with the same {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} and/or are engaged with open portions of {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} in proximity to the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}:
  
a. If a possible <GG14> {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} occurs (e.g., damage, {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}}, or tipping over), a judgment call will be made by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} within the context of <GG14> and <GG15> (just as it would if the interaction had occurred during the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}).
b. Incidental {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of <SG7> will not be penalized and will not automatically award the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} to the opponent as described by <GG13>. However, this allowance only applies when opposing {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are interacting with the same element.
c. Intentional, strategic, repeated, or egregious offenses, such as negatively impacting {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} that are not engaging with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}, may still be deemed a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <GG13>, <GG14>, <GG15>, <SG7>, {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, and / or <S1> at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion.
{{CALLOUT}} These gameplay elements are intended to be utilized by either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. This will inevitably result in {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}-on-{{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} interactions, both incidental and intentional. The overarching intent of <SG8> is for the vast majority of these interactions to result in no rule {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} and/or penalties for either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}, just as no rules {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} occur in 99% of Driver-controlled interactions.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG6>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<SG8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Engage with the Autonomous Line at your own risk.{{/BOLD}} Any {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} who engages with {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} and/or engages with an open portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} in proximity to the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} should be aware that opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may also choose to do the same. Per <GG12> and <GG13>, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are responsible for the actions of their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} at all times.
 During the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}, when {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} from opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} are both engaged with the same {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} and/or are engaged with open portions of {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} in proximity to the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}:
  
a. If a possible <GG14> {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} occurs (e.g., damage, {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}}, or tipping over), a judgment call will be made by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} within the context of <GG14> and <GG15> (just as it would if the interaction had occurred during the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}).
b. Incidental {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of <SG7> will not be penalized and will not automatically award the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} to the opponent as described by <GG13>. However, this allowance only applies when opposing {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are interacting with the same element.
c. Intentional, strategic, repeated, or egregious offenses, such as negatively impacting {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} that are not engaging with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}}, may still be deemed a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <GG13>, <GG14>, <GG15>, <SG7>, {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, and / or <S1> at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion.
{{CALLOUT}} These gameplay elements are intended to be utilized by either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. This will inevitably result in {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}-on-{{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} interactions, both incidental and intentional. The overarching intent of <SG8> is for the vast majority of these interactions to result in no rule {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} and/or penalties for either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}, just as no rules {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} occur in 99% of Driver-controlled interactions.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG6>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Match Loads may be introduced during the Match under certain conditions
rules.description: Match Loads may be introduced during the Match under certain conditions
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG9>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Match Loads may be introduced during the Match under certain conditions.{{/BOLD}} For the purpose of this rule, “introduce” refers to the moment when a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} has released a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} into a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}.
 During this action, a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members'{{/LINK}} hands may temporarily break the plane of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}. This momentary interaction is an exception to rule <GG4>. Excessive, unnecessary, or unsafe actions while introducing a {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} may be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <S1> and/or {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion.
 {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} may introduce {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} by placing them into either of the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to their {{LINK:#alliancestation}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} may only be added to {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
b. A {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} may not be contacted by a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} prior to being placed into a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} may only be removed from a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}} by a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, and only through the bottom opening of the {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}.
d. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that incidentally bounce out through the bottom opening of a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}} while being introduced should not be considered {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}.
e. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} can only be added to a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}} if there are no {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} partially or entirely within the orange portion of that {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: {{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
a. For the purposes of {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} calculations, each illegal {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} should be considered worth a value of 3 points. These values are not added to the actual score. If subtracting 3 points per illegal {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} from the the offending {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance's{{/LINK}} final score would change the outcome of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, then the <SG9> {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} should be considered {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}.
b. For the purposes of this rule, the following guidelines should be used for escalating repeated {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} to a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} during {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}:

  
  a. Six or more illegal {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Loads{{/LINK}} in a single {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}.
  b. Three or more {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} with at least one illegal {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} (i.e., the third {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and all subsequent {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} with a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}).
c. For the purposes of this rule, only {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} should be considered during {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}} (i.e., repeated {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} in {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}} do not compound to {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}}).

  
  a. Point B-1 does not explicitly apply during {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}, although the risk of illegal {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} becoming {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} still inherently increases as the quantity of {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Loads{{/LINK}} increases.
  b. Point B-2 does not apply during {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} The primary intent of clauses A & E are to prevent {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from being introduced in a way that overfills the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} (e.g., {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} extending well above the top of the {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}), thereby reducing potential <SG4> and <SG9> {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} resulting from {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} being removed/ejected from the top of the {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}. If a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} is not certain whether or not a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of clause E occurred, they should give the Team the benefit of the doubt and not record it as a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. This benefit of the doubt must be applied equally to all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} at that event. We don't intend this to be a rule that requires referees to closely watch every {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}. If there's brief overlap in the orange portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} as {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} are rapidly added to a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}, it probably shouldn't be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUG3>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Don’t reach inside enclosed sections of Goals, and no Goalkeeping
rules.description: Don’t reach inside enclosed sections of Goals, and no Goalkeeping
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG10>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Don’t reach inside enclosed sections of Goals, and no Goalkeeping.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are not permitted to directly contact {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that are fully within enclosed sections of {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goals{{/LINK}}, but may add or remove {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from open sections of {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} at any time during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
 A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can only reach into any open portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} to legally move {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} (e.g., into or out of the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} or the entire Goal). {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} is allowed during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. If a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} sees a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that is {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} of a Head-to-Head {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, that {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} should receive a verbal warning and must exit the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}.
 A {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} should loudly call out {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} and identify the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}(s) by color and/or pointing at them (e.g., “red {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}}” while pointing at a red {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that is engaged in {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}}).
 
 
a. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} should receive a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} if it takes no action to leave the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} after receiving a verbal warning, or is still in the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} after a brief period of time. The length of this period of time is subject to {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} judgement within the context of the {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} occurrence and the warned {{LINK:#robot}}Robot's{{/LINK}} actions (or lack thereof).
b. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that adds or removes {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} to or from the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} after the verbal warning and prior to exiting the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} should receive a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
c. After leaving the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must back up from the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} such that there is visible space between the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and the open portion of the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} (e.g., just lifting the “wing” isn’t enough to end the {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} call) before it can re-engage with an open portion of that {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that exits and reaches back into the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} after the verbal warning and prior to sufficiently backing away from the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} should receive a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes:
 {{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
a. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can receive multiple {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} for a single {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} call; for example, by remaining in the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} for an extended period after the verbal warning and then removing {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} as they depart.
b. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that remains in a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} after receiving a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} (e.g., an unpowered {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that ignores the {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} call) is subject to a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee's{{/LINK}} discretion.
c. A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} with one or two {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} should be considered {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} if their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} ties or wins the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} by 10 points or less.
d. For the purposes of this rule, the following guidelines should be used to escalate repeated Minor {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} to a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} in {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}:

  
  i. Three or more {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} by a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} in a single {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  ii. Three or more {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} with at least one {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} by a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} (i.e., the third {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and all subsequent {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} with a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}).

rules.title: Park Zones are protected during the endgame
rules.description: Park Zones are protected during the endgame
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<SG11>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Park Zones are protected during the endgame.{{/BOLD}} During the last 20 seconds of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may not directly or indirectly contact the other {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} or opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} that are partially or entirely within the vertical projection of that {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}.
 
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Notes: Intentional, strategic, egregious, or {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} will be considered {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: One Robot per Team
rules.description: One Robot per Team
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}One Robot per Team.{{/BOLD}} Each Team can only bring one (1) {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to a given event in the VEX V5 Robotics Competition. Though it is expected that {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will make changes to their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at the competition, a Team is limited to only one (1) {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at a given event, and a given {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may only be used by one (1) Team. A VEX {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, for the purposes of the VEX V5 Robotics Competition, has the following subsystems:
  
• Subsystem 1: Mobile robotic base including wheels, tracks, legs, or any other mechanism that allows the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to navigate the majority of the flat playing Field surface. For a stationary {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, the robotic base without wheels would be considered Subsystem 1.
• Subsystem 2: Power and control system that includes a legal VEX battery, a legal VEX control system, and associated motors for the mobile robotic base.
• Subsystem 3: Subsystem 3: Additional mechanisms (and associated motors) that allow manipulation of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} and interactions with {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} and other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. Given the above definitions, a minimum {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for use in any VEX V5 Robotics Competition event (including Skills Challenges) must consist of subsystems 1 and 2 above. Thus, if you are swapping out an entire subsystem 1 or 2, you have now created a second {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and have Violated this rule.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not compete with one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} while a second is being modified or assembled at a competition.
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not have an assembled second {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on hand at a competition that is used to repair or swap parts with the first {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not switch back and forth between multiple {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} during a competition. This includes using different {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} for {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}, and/or {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}.
d. Multiple {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not use the same {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. Once a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has competed under a given Team number at an event, it is “their” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}; no other {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may EVER compete with it.
{{CALLOUT}} The intent of <R1a>, <R1b>, and <R1c> is to ensure an unambiguous level playing Field for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are welcome (and encouraged) to improve or modify their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} between events, or to collaborate with other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to develop the best possible game solution.
 However, a Team who brings and/or competes with two separate {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} at the same tournament has diminished the efforts of a Team who spent extra design time making sure that their one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can accomplish all of the game’s tasks. A multi-Team organization that shares a single {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has diminished the efforts of a multi-Team organization who puts in the time, effort, and resources to undergo separate individual design processes and develop their own {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
 To help determine if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is a “separate {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}” or not, use the subsystem definitions found in <R1>. Above that, use common sense as referenced in <G3>. If you can place two {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} on a table next to each other, and they look like two separate legal/complete {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} (i.e., each has the 3 subsystems defined by <R1>), then they are two {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. Trying to decide if changing a screw, a wheel, or a microcontroller constitutes a separate {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is missing the intent and spirit of this rule.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR1>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Robots must represent the Team’s skill level
rules.description: Robots must represent the Team’s skill level
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots must represent the Team’s skill level.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be designed, built, and programmed by members of the Team. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are expected to mentor and teach design, building, and programming skills to the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} on the Team, but {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} may not design, build, or program that {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. See rules <G2>, <G4>, and <G5>.
 
{{CALLOUT}} In V5RC, we expect {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} to teach fundamental {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} principles like linkages, drive-trains, and manipulators, then allow the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to determine which designs to implement and build on their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
 Similarly, {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are encouraged to teach the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} how to code various functions involving applicable sensors and mechanisms, then have the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} program the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} from what they have learned.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Robots must pass inspection
rules.description: Robots must pass inspection
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots must pass inspection.{{/BOLD}} Every {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} will be required to pass a full inspection before being cleared to compete. This inspection will ensure that all {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules and regulations are met. Initial inspections will take place during Team registration/practice time. Noncompliance with any {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} design or construction rule will result in removal from {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at an event until the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is brought back into compliance, as described in the following subclauses.
  
a. Significant changes to a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, such as a partial or full swap of Subsystem 3, must be re-inspected before the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may compete again.
b. All possible functional {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} configurations must be inspected before being used in competition. This especially pertains to modular or swappable mechanisms (per <R1>) and {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} starting configurations/sizes (per <R5>).
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may be requested to submit to spot inspections by {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. Refusal to submit will result in {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is determined to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rule before a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} begins, the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} will be removed from the Field. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may remain at the Field so that the Team does not get assessed a “no-show” (per <GG2>).
d. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} which have not passed inspection (i.e., that may be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of one or more {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules) will not be permitted to play in any {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} until they have done so. <GG2> will apply to any {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} that occur until the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has passed inspection.
e. If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has passed inspection, but is later confirmed to be in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rule during or immediately following a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} by a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, they will be {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} from that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. This is the only {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} that will be affected; any prior {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} that have already been completed will not be revisited. <R3d> will apply until the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} is remedied and the Team is re-inspected.
f. All inspection rules are to be enforced within the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} within a given event. {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} legality at one event does not automatically imply legality at future events. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} which rely on “edge-case” interpretations of subjective rules, such as whether a decoration is “non-functional” or not, should expect additional scrutiny during inspection.
{{CALLOUT}} Events may wish to use “inspection markers” (e.g., zip tie or sticker) to identify {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} that have passed inspection at that event. Inspection markers are subject to all {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules, including legal materials and {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} size/expansion limits.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: There is a difference between accidentally and willfully violating a Robot rule
rules.description: There is a difference between accidentally and willfully violating a Robot rule
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}There is a difference between accidentally and willfully violating a Robot rule.{{/BOLD}} Any {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules, accidental or intentional, will result in a Team being unable to play until they pass inspection (per <R3d>).
 However, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who intentionally and/or knowingly circumvent or violate rules to gain an advantage over their fellow competitors are in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of the spirit and ethos of the competition. Any {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of this sort should be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} and/or the REC Foundation Code of Conduct. A Team that circumvents a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rule for a competitive advantage should receive an immediate {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} for the current {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and be reported to the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} for discussion with the REC Foundation Regional Support Manager. As a result of that discussion, the Team may be {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} from the event. The {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} should also be reported to the REC Foundation Rules and Conduct Committee following the event.

rules.title: Robots must fit within an 18” x 18” x 18” volume
rules.description: Robots must fit within an 18” x 18” x 18” volume

rules.title: Officially registered Team numbers must be displayed on Robot license plates
rules.description: Officially registered Team numbers must be displayed on Robot license plates
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Officially registered Team numbers must be displayed on Robot license plates.{{/BOLD}} To participate in an official VEX V5 Robotics Competition event, a Team must first register on RobotEvents.com and receive a V5RC Team number. This Team number must be displayed on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} using license plates. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may choose to use the official V5RC License Plate Kit, or may create their own using only legal materials.
  
a. License plates must be placed in fixed locations on exactly two (2) horizontally opposing sides of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and must remain visible, legible, and attached for the entirety of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. The top of a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not considered a “side” for these two license plates

  
  i. License plates should be mounted in locations that remain stationary on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (e.g, not on a rotating intake or flipping manipulator). The function of license plates is to identify {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} for referees, spectators, and other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}. Identification is harder when a license plate on a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} moves during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
b. License plates must be attached to the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} using materials that are legal for {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} construction. VEX IQ pins are no longer legal for mounting license plates on {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may only include license plates that match their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} color for the current {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., red {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must have only red plates installed for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}).
d. License plates are considered functional components, and must meet the requirements of all {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules.
e. Additional license plates cannot be used on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for any purpose.
f. Team numbers must be in white font, and clearly legible. Number/letter stickers are legal for use on license plates instead of or in addition to those in the V5RC License Plate Kit.
g. Custom license plates used to meet the requirements of <R6a> and <R6f> must be within the following size limits:

  
  i. Height: between 2.0 (50.8mm) and 2.5 inches (63.5mm)
  ii. Width: between 4.0 (101.6mm) and 4.5 inches (114.3mm)
  iii. Thickness: up to 0.25” (6.35mm)
{{CALLOUT}} Imagine the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} as a cube that’s sitting flat on a flat surface. License plates should be placed onto two (2) opposing faces of that cube (excluding the top and bottom).
 The intent of this rule is to make it immediately apparent to {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} and other event personnel which {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} and which Team each {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} belongs to, at all times. It will be at the full discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} and inspector at a given event to determine whether a given custom license plate satisfies the criteria listed in <R6>.
 {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} wishing to utilize custom plates should be prepared for the possibility of this judgment, and ensure that they are prepared to replace any custom parts with official VEX license plates if requested. Not bringing official replacement plates to an event will not be an acceptable reason for overlooking a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of one or more points in <R6>. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are encouraged to use an easily-read, sans-serif font (e.g., Arial).
 If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be removed from the Field based on this rule, <R3ci> applies and the Team should not be issued a “no-show.”
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 The intent of this rule is to make it immediately apparent to {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} and other event personnel which {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} and which Team each {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} belongs to, at all times. It will be at the full discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} and inspector at a given event to determine whether a given custom license plate satisfies the criteria listed in <R6>.
 {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} wishing to utilize custom plates should be prepared for the possibility of this judgment, and ensure that they are prepared to replace any custom parts with official VEX license plates if requested. Not bringing official replacement plates to an event will not be an acceptable reason for overlooking a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of one or more points in <R6>. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are encouraged to use an easily-read, sans-serif font (e.g., Arial).
 If a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be removed from the Field based on this rule, <R3ci> applies and the Team should not be issued a “no-show.”
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Let go of Blocks after the Match
rules.description: Let go of Blocks after the Match
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<R7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Let go of Blocks after the Match.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must be designed to permit easy removal of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from any mechanism without requiring the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to have power after a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<R7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Let go of Blocks after the Match.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must be designed to permit easy removal of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from any mechanism without requiring the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to have power after a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Robots have one Brain
rules.description: Robots have one Brain
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots have one Brain.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must ONLY use one (1) VEX V5 Robot Brain (276-4810). Any other microcontrollers or processing devices are not allowed, even as non-functional decorations.
 This includes microcontrollers that are part of other VEX product lines, such as VEX Cortex, VEX EXP, VEXpro, VEX CTE, VEX RCR, VEX IQ, VEX GO, or VEX Robotics by HEXBUG. This also includes devices that are unrelated to VEX, such as Raspberry Pi or Arduino devices.
  
a. V5 Robot Brain accessories (short flanges, long flanges, and the magnetic screen protector) are part of the V5 Robot Brain and are only legal for use on {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} as part of the V5 Robot Brain.
b. At events using V5 Smart Field Control System, the "Team Number" field in the Robot Brain must be set as the {{LINK:#team}}Team's{{/LINK}} registered number and letter (with no spaces). More information is availabile {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/21188629076116-Setting-the-Team-Number-for-the-VEX-Brain-in-VS-Code}}here{{/LINK}}.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR10> and <VUR12>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 This includes microcontrollers that are part of other VEX product lines, such as VEX Cortex, VEX EXP, VEXpro, VEX CTE, VEX RCR, VEX IQ, VEX GO, or VEX Robotics by HEXBUG. This also includes devices that are unrelated to VEX, such as Raspberry Pi or Arduino devices.

More information is availabile {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/21188629076116-Setting-the-Team-Number-for-the-VEX-Brain-in-VS-Code}}here{{/LINK}}.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR10> and <VUR12>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Keep the power button accessible
rules.description: Keep the power button accessible
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R9>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Keep the power button accessible.{{/BOLD}} The on/off button on the V5 Robot Brain and/or the Battery Cable connection on either the V5 Robot Brain or V5 Robot Battery must be accessible without moving or lifting the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. The V5 Brain screen must be easily visible during {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} inspection. Keeping the V5 Brain screen visible throughout a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is recommended but not required.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is in place to ensure the safety of both competitors and field staff. In the event that a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} needs to be quickly powered off—whether due to a malfunction, {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}}, or other safety concern—it is crucial that the power button and/or Robot Battery remains easily accessible. This allows competitors and/or field personnel to safely {{LINK:#disablement}}Disable{{/LINK}} the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} without putting their hands near moving parts or other hazards inside the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
 Additionally, keeping screens and indicator lights visible helps officials diagnose issues efficiently, minimizing downtime and ensuring a smooth competition experience. If the V5 Brain is accessible, Field volunteers can help {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} troubleshoot time-sensitive issues prior to a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, including switching between Bluetooth and VEXnet radio modes as needed, selecting programs on the V5 Brain in instances that prevent selection via the V5 Controller, etc. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will also have easier access during any needed <GG4> clause a interactions.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

{{BOLD}}<R9>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Keep the power button accessible.{{/BOLD}} The on/off button on the V5 Robot Brain and/or the Battery Cable connection on either the V5 Robot Brain or V5 Robot Battery must be accessible without moving or lifting the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. The V5 Brain screen must be easily visible during {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} inspection. Keeping the V5 Brain screen visible throughout a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is recommended but not required.

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Firmware
rules.description: Firmware
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R10>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Firmware.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must use VEXos version 1.1.5 or newer, found at {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/firmware}}https://link.vex.com/firmware{{/LINK}}. Custom firmware modifications are not permitted.
  
a. The minimum version requirement is subject to change over the course of the season.
b. When the minimum version is updated, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} have a two week (14 calendar day) grace period from the time the minimum version is changed to update their firmware to the latest minimum version.
c. VEX reserves the right to deem any firmware update critical, and remove the allowable grace period.
d. Beta firmware, which includes any firmware version that ends with the letter ‘b’, is not legal for use in competition.

{{BOLD}}<R10>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Firmware.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must use VEXos version 1.1.5 or newer, found at {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/firmware}}https://link.vex.com/firmware{{/LINK}}. Custom firmware modifications are not permitted.
  
a. The minimum version requirement is subject to change over the course of the season.
b. When the minimum version is updated, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} have a two week (14 calendar day) grace period from the time the minimum version is changed to update their firmware to the latest minimum version.
c. VEX reserves the right to deem any firmware update critical, and remove the allowable grace period.
d. Beta firmware, which includes any firmware version that ends with the letter ‘b’, is not legal for use in competition.

rules.title: Use a “Competition Template” for programming
rules.description: Use a “Competition Template” for programming
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R11>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Use a “Competition Template” for programming.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be programmed to follow control directions provided by the VEXnet Field Controllers or Smart Field Control system.
 During the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} will not be allowed to use their V5 Controllers. As such, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are responsible for programming their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} with custom software if they want to perform in the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}.
 This may be tested in inspection, where {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may be required to pass a functional “enable/disable” test. For more information on this, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should consult the help guides produced by the developers of their chosen programming software.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<R11>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Use a “Competition Template” for programming.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be programmed to follow control directions provided by the VEXnet Field Controllers or Smart Field Control system.
 During the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} will not be allowed to use their V5 Controllers. As such, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are responsible for programming their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} with custom software if they want to perform in the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}.
 This may be tested in inspection, where {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may be required to pass a functional “enable/disable” test. For more information on this, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should consult the help guides produced by the developers of their chosen programming software.

rules.title: Motors are limited
rules.description: Motors are limited
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R12>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Motors are limited.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use any combination of VEX V5 Smart Motors (11W) (276-4840) and Smart Motors (5.5W) (276-4842), within the following criteria:
  
a. The combined power of all motors (11W & 5.5W) must not exceed 88W. This limit applies to all motors on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, even those which are not plugged in.
b. V5 Smart Motors and EXP Smart Motors connected to Smart Ports are the only motors that may be used with a V5 Robot Brain. The 3-wire ports may not be used to control motors of any kind.
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR11>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<R12>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Motors are limited.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use any combination of VEX V5 Smart Motors (11W) (276-4840) and Smart Motors (5.5W) (276-4842), within the following criteria:
  
a. The combined power of all motors (11W & 5.5W) must not exceed 88W. This limit applies to all motors on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, even those which are not plugged in.
b. V5 Smart Motors and EXP Smart Motors connected to Smart Ports are the only motors that may be used with a V5 Robot Brain. The 3-wire ports may not be used to control motors of any kind.
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR11>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Electrical power comes from VEX batteries only
rules.description: Electrical power comes from VEX batteries only
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R13>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Electrical power comes from VEX batteries only.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use one (1) V5 Robot Battery (276-4811) to power the V5 Robot Brain.
  
a. No other sources of electrical power are permitted, unless used as part of a non-functional decoration per <R24e>.
b. There are no legal power expanders for the V5 Robot Battery.
c. V5 Robot Batteries may only be charged by a V5 Robot Battery Charger (276-4812 or 276-4841).
d. V5 Controllers (276-4820) may only be powered by their internal rechargeable battery.

  
  i. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are permitted to have an external power source (such as a rechargeable battery pack) plugged into their V5 Controller during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, provided that this power source is connected safely and does not violate any other rules, such as <R28>.
  ii. Some events may choose to provide Field power for V5 Controllers. If this is provided for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} at the event, then this is a legal power source for the V5 Controllers.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR12>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:

a. No other sources of electrical power are permitted, unless used as part of a non-functional decoration per <R24e>.
b. There are no legal power expanders for the V5 Robot Battery.
c. V5 Robot Batteries may only be charged by a V5 Robot Battery Charger (276-4812 or 276-4841).
d. V5 Controllers (276-4820) may only be powered by their internal rechargeable battery.

  
  i. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are permitted to have an external power source (such as a rechargeable battery pack) plugged into their V5 Controller during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, provided that this power source is connected safely and does not violate any other rules, such as <R28>.
  ii. Some events may choose to provide Field power for V5 Controllers. If this is provided for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} at the event, then this is a legal power source for the V5 Controllers.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR12>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Robots use VEXnet
rules.description: Robots use VEXnet
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R14>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots use VEXnet.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must ONLY utilize the VEXnet system for all wireless {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} communication.
  
a. Electronics from the Cortex, VEX EXP, VEX CTE. VEXpro, VEX RCR, VEXplorer, VEX IQ, VEX GO, or VEX Robotics by HEXBUG product line are prohibited unless otherwise noted in <R17>.
b. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are permitted to use the Bluetooth® capabilities of the V5 Robot Brain and/or V5 Controller in Team pits, practice Fields, and {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}. However, VEXnet must be used for wireless communication during head-to-head {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are permitted to use the Wi-Fi capabilities of the Vision Sensor in Team pits or outside of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}. However, the Vision Sensor must have its wireless transmitting functionality disabled during {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR2>, <VUR10> and <VUR12>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

a. Electronics from the Cortex, VEX EXP, VEX CTE. VEXpro, VEX RCR, VEXplorer, VEX IQ, VEX GO, or VEX Robotics by HEXBUG product line are prohibited unless otherwise noted in <R17>.

c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are permitted to use the Wi-Fi capabilities of the Vision Sensor in Team pits or outside of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}. However, the Vision Sensor must have its wireless transmitting functionality disabled during {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR2>, <VUR10> and <VUR12>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Give the radio some space
rules.description: Give the radio some space

rules.title: One or two Controllers per Robot
rules.description: One or two Controllers per Robot
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R16>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}One or two Controllers per Robot.{{/BOLD}} No more than two (2) VEX V5 Controllers may control a single {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
  
a. No physical or electrical modification of these Controllers is allowed under any circumstances.

  
  i. Attachments which assist the {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} in holding or manipulating buttons/joysticks on the V5 Controller are permitted, provided that they do not involve direct physical or electrical modification of the Controller itself.
b. No other methods of controlling the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (light, sound, etc.) are permissible.

  
  i. Using sensor feedback to augment driver control (such as motor encoders or the Vision Sensor) is permitted.

b. No other methods of controlling the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (light, sound, etc.) are permissible.

  
  i. Using sensor feedback to augment driver control (such as motor encoders or the Vision Sensor) is permitted.

rules.title: Robots are built from the VEX V5 system
rules.description: Robots are built from the VEX V5 system
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R17>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robots are built from the VEX V5 system.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may be built ONLY using official VEX V5 components, unless otherwise specifically noted within these rules.
 All legal V5 components are listed in the {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/V5-Competition-Legal-Parts}}VEX V5 Robotics Competition Legal Parts List{{/LINK}}.
 Note: This new document is currently subject to frequent updates; contact {{LINK:/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection}}[email protected]{{/LINK}} or post in the official Q&A system with questions about this document and legal components.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR2>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: New VEX parts are legal
rules.description: New VEX parts are legal
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R18>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}New VEX parts are legal.{{/BOLD}} Additional VEX components released during the competition season on {{LINK:https://www.vexrobotics.com}}www.vexrobotics.com{{/LINK}} are considered legal for use unless otherwise noted.
 Some “new” components may have certain restrictions placed on them upon their release. These restrictions will be documented in the official Q&A, in a Game Manual update, or on their respective product web pages.

rules.title: Prohibited Items
rules.description: Prohibited Items
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R19>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Prohibited Items. The following types of mechanisms and components are NOT allowed.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. Those that could potentially damage {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}.
b. Those that could potentially damage other competing {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
c. Those that pose an unnecessary risk of {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}} with other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}.
d. Those that could pose a potential safety hazard to {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}}, event staff, or other humans.
e. Products from the VEXpro, VEX EXP, VEX IQ, VEX GO, VEX 123, VEX CTE, VEX AIM, VEX AIR, or VEX Robotics by HEXBUG* product lines, unless specifically allowed by a clause of <R17> or “cross-listed” as part of the VEX V5 Product lines (see <R17a>). {{BOLD}}{{ITALIC}}* The HEXBUG brand is a registered trademark belonging to Spin Master Corp{{/ITALIC}}
{{/BOLD}}
f. The following electronics from the VEX Cortex control system.
g. Components that are unique to the V5 Workcell product line. This includes the following.
h. Components obtained from the V5 beta program, including V5 beta firmware.

  
  i. All V5 beta hardware can be identified by its lighter gray pre-production color. Robot Brains, Robot Batteries, Controllers, and Vision Sensors from the V5 beta have a “BETA TEST” stamp on them. Smart Motors and Radios do not have this stamp, but can still be identified by color.
i. Components from the VEXplorer kit that are not found in modern VEX V5 kits. These include (but may not be limited to) electronics, wheels, non-standard gears, and plastic connectors.
j. Standalone VEX Smart Field Controller Brains (SKU 276-7577).
k. VEX apparel, competition support materials, packaging, or other non-{{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} products.
l. 3D printed {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} parts for any purpose, including non-functional decorations and license plates.
m. Speakers and other audio devices that create sound are not permitted.
{{CALLOUT}} 3D printed Controller attachments, 3D printed {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} alignment tools, and/or other custom 3D printed tools that do not go onto the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} are not considered {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} parts, and may be legal for use if they meet the requirements of other pertinent rules.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rules <VUR2>, <VUR5>, <VUR6>, & <VUR13>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
