a. Those that could potentially damage {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}.
b. Those that could potentially damage other competing {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
c. Those that pose an unnecessary risk of {{LINK:#entangle}}Entanglement{{/LINK}} with other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}.
d. Those that could pose a potential safety hazard to {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}}, event staff, or other humans.

rules.title: Certain non-VEX components are allowed
rules.description: Certain non-VEX components are allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R20>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Certain non-VEX components are allowed.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are allowed the following additional “non-VEX” components:
  
a. Any material strictly used as a color filter or a color marker for a legal sensor, such as the VEX Light Sensor or the VEX V5 Vision Sensor.
b. Any non-aerosol-based grease or lubricating compound, when used in extreme moderation on surfaces and locations that do NOT contact the playing Field walls, foam Field tiles, {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, or other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. Grease or lubricant applied directly to V5 Smart Motors or Smart Motor cartridges is prohibited.
c. Anti-static compound, when used in extreme moderation (i.e., such that it does not leave residue on {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, or other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}).
d. Hot glue when used to secure cable connections.
e. An unlimited amount of rope/string, no thicker than 1/4” (6.35mm).
f. Commercially available items used solely for bundling or wrapping of 2-wire, 3-wire, 4-wire, or V5 Smart Cables, and/or pneumatic tubing are allowed. These items must solely be used for the purposes of cable/tubing protection, organization, or management. This includes but is not limited to electrical tape, cable carrier, cable track, etc. It is up to inspectors to determine whether a component is serving a function beyond protecting and managing cables and tubing.
g. Rubber bands no larger than 7.5” long and 0.25” wide.
h. Pneumatic components with identical SMC manufacturer part numbers to those included in the {{LINK:https://link.vex.com/V5-Competition-Legal-Parts}}VEX V5 Robotics Competition Legal Parts List{{/LINK}}. For more detail regarding legal pneumatic components, see the {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/17984679227156-Legal-Pneumatics-for-Use-in-the-VEX-Robotics-Competition-VRC}}Legal VEX Pneumatics Summary document{{/LINK}}.
i. Plastic zip ties no larger than 12” long and 0.25” wide.
j. A Micro SD card installed in the V5 Robot Brain.
k. Aerosol-based cooling/freeze spray may be used to assist in cooling motors. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} using freeze spray or similar products in ways that may reasonably be deemed unsafe could be subject to <S1> {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}.
l. Cleaners, disinfectants, and/or sanitizers may be used to assist in cleaning {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, parts, components, etc. VEX Robotics recommends {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/360047126751-VEX-Product-Disinfecting-Guide}}the following procedures for cleaning/disinfecting/sanitizing robot parts{{/LINK}}.
m. Anti-slip drawer liner with similar nominal dimensions to VEX Anti-Slip Mat (275-0120 or 275-0121). The pattern of the matting should be similar to VEX Anti-Slip Mat, and there should be no additional performance gained by using non-VEX anti-slip drawer liner (i.e., no additional elasticity, adhesive backing, etc.). No piece of anti-slip drawer liner can be larger than 12” x 15” and all pieces must be black in color.
n. See rules <R21> through <R25> for additional legal non-VEX components.
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rules <VUR3>, <VUR4>, <VUR7>, <VUR8>, <VUR9>, <VUR12>, <VUR14> & <VUR15>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

a. Any material strictly used as a color filter or a color marker for a legal sensor, such as the VEX Light Sensor or the VEX V5 Vision Sensor.
b. rules.title: Custom V5 Smart Cables are allowed
rules.description: Custom V5 Smart Cables are allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R21>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Custom V5 Smart Cables are allowed.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who create custom cables acknowledge that incorrect wiring may have undesired results.
  
a. Official V5 Smart Cable Stock must be used.
b. Use of non-VEX 4P4C connectors and 4P4C crimping tools is permissible.
c. V5 Smart Cables may only be used for connecting legal electronic devices to the V5 Robot Brain.

c. V5 Smart Cables may only be used for connecting legal electronic devices to the V5 Robot Brain.

rules.title: A limited amount of tape is allowed
rules.description: A limited amount of tape is allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R22>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}A limited amount of tape is allowed.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use a small amount of tape for the following purposes:
  
a. To secure any connection between the ends of two (2) VEX cables.
b. To label wires and motors.
c. To prevent leaks on the threaded portions of pneumatic fittings. This is the only acceptable use of Teflon tape.
d. In any other application that would be considered a “non-functional decoration” per <R24>.
e. As an aglet at the end of rope/string to prevent fraying.
rules.completeText:

c. To prevent leaks on the threaded portions of pneumatic fittings. This is the only acceptable use of Teflon tape.
d. In any other application that would be considered a “non-functional decoration” per <R24>.
e. As an aglet at the end of rope/string to prevent fraying.

rules.title: Certain non-VEX fasteners are allowed
rules.description: Certain non-VEX fasteners are allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R23>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Certain non-VEX fasteners are allowed.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use the following commercially available hardware:
  
a. #4, #6, #8, M3, M3.5, or M4 screws up to 2.5” (63.5 mm) long, and M2.5 x 8mm screws.
b. Shoulder screws with a shoulder length no longer than 0.20” and a diameter no larger than 0.176”.
c. Any commercially available nut, washer, standoff, and/or non-threaded spacer up to 2.5” (63.5mm) long which fits these screws.
{{CALLOUT}} The intent of the rule is to allow {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to purchase their own commodity hardware without introducing additional functionality not found in standard VEX equipment. It is up to inspectors to determine whether the non-VEX hardware has introduced additional functionality or not.
 For the purposes of this rule, weight savings is not considered additional functionality.
 If a key component of a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} design relies upon convincing an inspector that a specialized component is “technically a screw,” it is probably outside of the spirit and intent of this rule.
 All specific dimensions listed in this rule are intended to be ‘nominal’ references to hardware sizes found within the VEX V5 product line and/or their metric equivalents.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR9>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Visual decorations are allowed
rules.description: Visual decorations are allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R24>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Visual decorations are allowed.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may add non-functional decorations, provided that they do not affect {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} performance in any significant way or affect the outcome of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. These decorations must be in the spirit of the competition. Inspectors and {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} will have final say in what is considered “non-functional.” Unless otherwise specified below, non-functional decorations are governed by all standard {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} rules.
 To be considered “non-functional,” any guards, decals, or other decorations must be backed by legal materials that provide the same functionality. For example, if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has a giant decal that prevents {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from falling out of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, the decal must be backed by VEX material that would also prevent the {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from falling out. A simple way to check this is to determine if removing the decoration would impact the performance of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} in any way.
  
a. Anodizing and painting of parts is considered a legal nonfunctional decoration.
  Note: As of October 1, 2024, anodizing or color changing of official VEX Robotics parts (such as painting, etc., which changes the original appearance of those parts) is no longer allowed in events in mainland China.

  
a. Small cameras are permitted as non-functional decorations, provided that any transmitting functions or wireless communications are disabled. Unusually large cameras being used as ballast are not permitted.
b. VEX electronics may not be used as non-functional decorations.
c. Decorations that visually mimic {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, or that could otherwise interfere with an opponent’s Vision Sensor, are considered functional and are not permitted. The Inspector and {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} will make the final decision on whether a given decoration or mechanism violates this rule.
d. Internal power sources (e.g., for a small blinking light) are permitted, provided that no other rules are violated and this source only provides power to the non-functional decoration (i.e., does not directly or indirectly influence any functional portions of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}).
e. Decorations which provide feedback to the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} (e.g., by influencing legal sensors) would be considered “functional,” and are not permitted.
f. Decorations which provide visual feedback to {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} (e.g., decorative lighting) are permitted, provided that they do not violate any other rules and serve no other function (e.g., structural support).

rules.title: A limited amount of custom plastic is allowed
rules.description: A limited amount of custom plastic is allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R25>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}A limited amount of custom plastic is allowed.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use custom-made pieces cut from certain types of non-shattering plastic, up to 0.070” thick.
  
a. Each {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is limited to a maximum of 12 individual pieces cut from non-shattering plastic. This includes non-shattering plastic used in non-functional decorations.
b. Each individual piece of non-shattering plastic cannot be larger than 4” x 8” x 0.070”.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must present and display ALL non-shattering plastic parts during inspection.

  
  i. Inspectors will verify the total number of plastic pieces. They may use dry-erase markers or other forms or temporary marking to aid in counting.
  ii. Inspectors will verify that no non-shattering piece exceeds the size limitation.
d. Plastic may be mechanically altered by cutting, drilling, bending, etc. It cannot be chemically treated, melted, or cast. Heating non-shattering plastic to aid in bending is acceptable..
e. Legal plastic types are polycarbonate (Lexan), acetal monopolymer (Delrin), acetal copolymer (Acetron GP), POM (acetal), ABS, PEEK, PET, HDPE, LDPE, Nylon (all grades), Polypropylene, PTFE, and FEP.
f. Shattering plastic, such as PMMA (also called Plexiglass, Acrylic, or Perspex), is prohibited.
g. Plastic sheets sold by VEX are considered “plastic” in the context of this rule, and are subject to the same limitations as “off-the-shelf” plastic sheets. Examples include the 276-8340 PET sheets, and the 217-6626 / 217-6627 polycarbonate sheets.
h. This rule does not apply to 3D printed plastic parts. 3D printed parts are not permitted in the VEX V5 Robotics Competition for any purpose, including non-functional decorations. Note: {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are strongly encouraged to provide inspectors with 1:1 scale drawings, identical spares, or 1:1 scale tracings of their non-shattering plastic pieces to aid in inspection. Drawings and tracings should accurately reflect ALL shapes and dimensions of each piece.
 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rules <VUR3> & <VUR4>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Pneumatics are limited
rules.description: Pneumatics are limited
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R26>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Pneumatics are limited.{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} pneumatic subsystem must satisfy all of the following criteria:

  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use a maximum of two (2) legal VEX pneumatic air reservoirs on a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. The Air Tank 200mL (included in the 276-8750 V5 Pneumatics Kit) and the legacy (pre-2023) reservoir are both considered legal reservoirs.
b. Pneumatic devices may be charged to a maximum of 100 psi.
c. The compressed air contained inside a pneumatic subsystem can only be used to actuate legal pneumatic devices (e.g., cylinders).
  Note: From a rules perspective, parts found in the V5 Pneumatics Kit (276-8750) and legacy (pre-2023) pneumatic parts may be used interchangeably. A Legal Pneumatics summary can be found {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/17984679227156-Legal-Pneumatics-for-Use-in-the-VEX-Robotics-Competition-VRC-}}in the VEX Library{{/LINK}}, which includes additional pneumatics information.
 
{{CALLOUT}} The intent of <R26a> and <R26b> is to limit {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} to the air pressure stored in two reservoir tanks, as well as the normal working air pressure contained in their pneumatic cylinders and tubing on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not use other elements for the purposes of storing or generating air pressure.
 Using cylinders or additional pneumatic tubing solely for additional storage is in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of the spirit of this rule. Similarly, using pneumatic cylinders and/or tubing without any air reservoirs is also in {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of the spirit of this rule.
 The intent of <R26c> is to ensure that pneumatics are being used safely. Pressurized systems, such as a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} pneumatic subsystem, have the potential to be dangerous if used incorrectly. This rule ensures the safety of participants, and prevents potentially unsafe uses in the future.
 Another way of thinking of <R26c> is that “pneumatics should only be used with pneumatics.” {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should not use compressed air as a means of actuating non-pneumatic devices such as screws, nuts, etc. For example, pulling a pin with a pneumatic cylinder is okay, but using air to actuate the pin itself is not.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUR14>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Most modifications to non-electrical components are allowed
rules.description: Most modifications to non-electrical components are allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R27>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Most modifications to non-electrical components are allowed.{{/BOLD}} Physical modifications, such as bending or cutting, of legal metal structure or plastic components are permitted.
  
a. Internal or external mechanical repairs of VEX Limit and Bumper switches are permitted.

  
  i. Modifying the metal arm on the Limit Switch is permitted.
  ii. Using components from these devices in other applications is prohibited.
b. Metallurgical modifications that change fundamental material properties, such as heat treating or melting, are not permitted.
c. Pneumatic tubing may be cut to desired lengths.
d. Fusing/melting the end of legal nylon rope/string (see <R20e>) to prevent fraying is permitted.
e. Welding, soldering, brazing, gluing, or attaching parts to each other in any way that is not provided within the VEX platform is not permitted. Rule <R20d> is an exception to this rule.
f. Mechanical fasteners may be secured using Loctite or a similar thread-locking product. This may ONLY be used for securing hardware, such as screws and nuts.
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rules <VUR3>, <VUR12> & <VUR13>
 {{/CALLOUT}}

  i. Modifying the metal arm on the Limit Switch is permitted.
  ii. Using components from these devices in other applications is prohibited.
b. Metallurgical modifications that change fundamental material properties, such as heat treating or melting, are not permitted.

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: No modifications to electronic or pneumatic components are allowed
rules.description: No modifications to electronic or pneumatic components are allowed
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<R28>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}No modifications to electronic or pneumatic components are allowed.{{/BOLD}} Motors (including the V5 Smart Motor firmware), microcontrollers (including V5 Robot Brain firmware), cables, sensors, controllers, battery packs, reservoirs, solenoids, pneumatic cylinders, and any other electrical or pneumatics component of the VEX platform may NOT be altered from their original state in ANY way.
 
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may make the following modifications to the V5 Smart Motor (11W)’s user-serviceable features. This list is all-inclusive; {{BOLD}}no other modifications are permitted{{/BOLD}}. Where applicable, the components listed below (in the specific applications listed below) are permissible exceptions to <R20>.

  
  i. Replacing the gear cartridge with other official cartridges.
  ii. Removing or replacing the screws from the V5 Smart Motor Cap (276-6780).
  iii. Removing or replacing the threaded mounting inserts (276-6781).
  iv. Aesthetic/non-functional labeling (e.g., markers, stickers, paint, etc.).
b. V5 Smart Motors (11W) must use an official VEX V5 gear cartridge. For the purposes of this rule, the gear cartridges found within the V5 Smart Motor are considered “part of the motor.” Therefore, any physical or functional modifications to official gear cartridges is not permitted. V5 Smart Motors (11W) may only use official VEX motor cartridges
c. For the purposes of this rule, the V5 Smart Motor Cap is not considered “part of the motor.” Therefore, <R27> applies.
d. External wires on VEX 2-wire or 3-wire electrical components may be repaired by soldering or using twist/crimp connectors, electrical tape, or shrink tubing such that the original functionality and length are not modified in any way.
  i. Wire used in repairs must be identical to VEX wire.
  ii. Teams make these repairs at their own risk; incorrect wiring may have undesired results.
e. V5 Robot Brain accessories (short flanges, long flanges, and the magnetic screen protector) are considered "part of the V5 Robot Brain" and cannot be modified.

rules.title: Scoring Robot Skills Matches
rules.description: Scoring Robot Skills Matches
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Scoring Robot Skills Matches.{{/BOLD}} For each {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are awarded a score based on the following rules and scoring table:
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will receive points for all {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, regardless of color.
  i. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is not considered {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} if it is in contact with a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
b. A {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} is considered filled if it ends the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} containing its maximum number of {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, and if all {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} in that {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} are the same color (e.g., all are blue, or all are red).

  
  i. In a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}, a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} must contain at least seven (7) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} to be considered filled.
  ii. A {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} must include one or more of the following to be considered filled in a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}:

  
    a. Three {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the same color fully contained between the inner edges of the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} tape lines.
    b. Four {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the same color fully contained between the outer edges of the {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} tape lines.
c. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will earn points for a cleared {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} if no {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} are in contact with the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} inside the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
d. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will earn points for a cleared {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}} if no {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} are within that {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
e. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will earn points for a {{LINK:#parked}}Parked{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} if the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} has moved during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, is within the red {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}, and meets all criteria of rule <SC4> at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Robot and Field setup for Skills Matches
rules.description: Robot and Field setup for Skills Matches
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robot and Field setup for Skills Matches.{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} and Field are set up the same as for a Head-to-Head {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (e.g., the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must meet the requirements of <SG1>), with the following modifications:
  
a. In {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, the VEX GPS code strip must be installed on the Field.
b. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must start the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} in a legal starting position for the red {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
c. All {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} must remain in the red {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} for the duration of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
d. One red {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} must be used as a Preload in accordance with <SG5>.
e. Revised {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} layout. 36 {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in unscored positions on the Field and 24 {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} begin in the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}}, as shown in Figure RSC3-1.
f. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may move freely about the Field after the start of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
g. {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} do not include {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rules <VURS1>, <VURS2> & <VURS3>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Skills Stop Time
rules.description: Skills Stop Time
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<RSC4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Skills Stop Time.{{/BOLD}} If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} wishes to end their {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} early, they may elect to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}. This is used as a tiebreaker for Robot Skills Challenge rankings. A {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} does not affect a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} score for a given {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who intend to attempt a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} must “opt-in” by verbally confirming with the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}} prior to the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}. If no notification is given prior to the start of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, then the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} forfeits their option to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. This conversation should include informing the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}} which {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} will signal the stop. The {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} may only be ended early by a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  ii. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} runs multiple {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} in a row, they must reconfirm their {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} choice with the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}} prior to each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  iii. Any questions regarding a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} should be reviewed and settled immediately following the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. <T1> and <T3> apply to {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.
b. If the event is utilizing a V5 Robot Brain or the TM Mobile app for Robot Skills Challenge field control, a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} may elect to start and stop their own {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. This V5 Robot Brain or other device running the TM Mobile app will be used to start the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} (i.e., “enable” the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}), end the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., “{{LINK:#disablement}}Disable{{/LINK}}” the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}), and display the official {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} to be recorded.
  ii. This V5 Robot Brain must be running the official field control user program.
  iii. For more information regarding the use of a V5 Robot Brain for Robot Skills Challenge field control, and to download the official field control user program, {{LINK:https://kb.vex.com/hc/en-us/articles/360044865591-Using-a-V5-Robot-Brain-for-Robot-Skills-Challenge-Field-Control}}visit this VEX Knowledge Base article{{/LINK}}.
  iv. For more information regarding the use of TM Mobile for field control, {{LINK:https://www.roboticseducation.org/app/tm-mobile/}}see the Tournament Manager documentation{{/LINK}}.
c. At events which do not have a V5 Robot Brain or the TM Mobile App available for Robot Skills Challenge field control, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} and field staff must agree prior to the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} on the signal that will be used to end the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early.

  
  i. As noted in the definition of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}, the moment when the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} ends early is defined as the moment when the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is “{{LINK:#disablement}}Disabled{{/LINK}}” by the field control system.
  ii. The agreed-upon signal must be both verbal and visual, such as {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} crossing their arms in an “X” or placing their V5 Controller(s) on the ground.
  iii. The signal must be given by a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}} who is standing in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}}.
  iv. It is recommended that {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} also provide verbal notice that they are approaching their {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}, such as by counting out “3-2-1-stop.”
d. It is at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion which method will be used to record {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} at a given event. The chosen method must be communicated prior to the start of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} (such as during an event meeting), and made equally available to all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}.

  
  i. If an event intends to use a manual timekeeping method, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may not bring their own V5 Robot Brain just for use during their own {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}.
  ii. If an event intends to utilize a V5 Robot Brain, all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must use the same V5 Robot Brain for all {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} on a given Field.
  iii. If an event is using multiple Fields for {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, the same method must be used at all Fields, as described in rule {{LINK:_idTextAnchor161}}<T21>{{/LINK}}. Multiple V5 Robot Brains may be used as needed (e.g., a “Field 1 Brain” and a “Field 2 Brain”).
  iv. The default “Drive” program accessed from a V5 Controller is intended for practice only, and may not be used for an official {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}.
e. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} chooses to utilize/record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}, the 5-second grace period described in rule <SC1> does not apply.

rules.title: Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and Robot ruling decisions during the competition
rules.description: Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and Robot ruling decisions during the competition
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and Robot ruling decisions during the competition.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} score the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and may serve as observers or advisers for {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}, but may not determine any {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} directly.
b. When issuing a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}} to a Team, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must provide the rule number of the specific rule that has been Violated, and must record the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} on the Match Anomaly Log.
c. {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} require additional escalation beyond the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} initial ruling, including (but not limited to) investigation by RECF representatives.
d. {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} may not overrule a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} gameplay or {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} decisions.
e. Every {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Match{{/LINK}} must be watched by a certified {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} may only watch one {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} at a time; if multiple {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} are happening simultaneously on separate {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, each Field must have its own {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must follow the rules in this game manual and the Q&A, and must make rulings consistent with the intent of the game manual and Q&A.
f. At a minimum, every {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} must be watched by a trained {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}}, who may only watch one {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} at a time. If multiple {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} are happening simultaneously on separate {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, each {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} must have its own {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}}. A certified {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} must be available at the event to explain a rule, {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}, or other penalty to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} as needed in support of the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} at skills {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}.
{{CALLOUT}} Note from the VEX GDC: The rules contained in this Game Manual are written to be enforced by human {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. Many rules have “black-and-white” criteria that can be easily checked. However, some rulings will rely on a judgment call from this human {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. In these cases, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} will make their calls based on what they and the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} saw, what guidance is provided by their official support materials (the Game Manual and the Q&A), and most crucially, the context of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in question.
 The VEX V5 Robotics Competition does not have video replay, our {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} do not have absolute sensors to count scores, and most events do not have the resources for an extensive review conference between each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
 When an ambiguous rule results in a controversial call, there is a natural instinct to wonder what the “right” ruling “should have been,” or what the GDC “would have ruled.” This is ultimately an irrelevant question; our answer is that when a rule specifies “{{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion” (or similar), then the “right” call is the one made by a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} in the moment. The VEX GDC designs games, and writes rules, with this expectation (constraint) in mind.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<T1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and Robot ruling decisions during the competition.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} score the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and may serve as observers or advisers for {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}, but may not determine any {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} directly.
b. When issuing a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}} to a Team, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must provide the rule number of the specific rule that has been Violated, and must record the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} on the Match Anomaly Log.
c. {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} require additional escalation beyond the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} initial ruling, including (but not limited to) investigation by RECF representatives.
d. {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} may not overrule a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} gameplay or {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} decisions.
e. Every {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Match{{/LINK}} must be watched by a certified {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} may only watch one {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} at a time; if multiple {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} are happening simultaneously on separate {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, each Field must have its own {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must follow the rules in this game manual and the Q&A, and must make rulings consistent with the intent of the game manual and Q&A.
f. At a minimum, every {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} must be watched by a trained {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}}, who may only watch one {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} at a time. If multiple {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} are happening simultaneously on separate {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, each {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} must have its own {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/LINK}}. A certified {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} must be available at the event to explain a rule, {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}, or other penalty to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} as needed in support of the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} at skills {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}.

Many rules have “black-and-white” criteria that can be easily checked. However, some rulings will rely on a judgment call from this human {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}. In these cases, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} will make their calls based on what they and the {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} saw, what guidance is provided by their official support materials (the Game Manual and the Q&A), and most crucially, the context of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in question.

The VEX GDC designs games, and writes rules, with this expectation (constraint) in mind.

rules.title: Head Referees must be qualified
rules.description: Head Referees must be qualified
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Head Referees must be qualified.{{/BOLD}} V5RC {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} must have all of the following qualifications:
  
a. Be at least 20 years of age.
b. Be approved by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}.
c. Be an REC Foundation Certified V5RC {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} for the current season. {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/14527449656087-REC-Foundation-Volunteer-Certifications}}Visit the REC Library for more details.{{/LINK}}
d. Cannot be the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} or a Judge Advisor for the event.
  
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} must be at least 15 years of age, and must be approved by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
 
{{CALLOUT}}{{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} should demonstrate the following attributes:
  
• Thorough knowledge of the current game and rules of play
• Effective decision-making skills
• Attention to detail
• Ability to work effectively as a member of a team
• Ability to be confident and assertive when necessary
• Strong communication and diplomacy skills {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Drive Team Members are permitted to immediately appeal a Head Referee’s ruling
rules.description: Drive Team Members are permitted to immediately appeal a Head Referee’s ruling
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Drive Team Members are permitted to immediately appeal a Head Referee’s ruling.{{/BOLD}} If {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} wish to dispute a score or ruling, they must stay in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} until the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} talks with them. The {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} may choose to meet with the {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} at another location and/or at a later time so that the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} has time to reference materials or resources to help with the decision. Once the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} announces that their decision has been made final, the issue is over and no more appeals may be made (See rule <T1>).
  
a. Referees are not permitted to review any photo or video {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} recordings when determining a score or ruling.
b. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} are the only individuals permitted to explain a rule, {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}, or other penalty to the {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in a Head-to-Head {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should never consult other field personnel, including {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}}, regarding a ruling clarification. Communication and conflict resolution skills are an important life skill for {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to practice and learn. In VEX V5 Robotics Competitions, we expect {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} to practice proper conflict resolution using the proper chain of command. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of this rule may be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} and/or the Code of Conduct.
 
{{CALLOUT}} Some events may choose to utilize a “question box” or other designated location for discussions with {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. Offering a “question box” is within the discretion of the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, and may act as an alternate option for asking {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} to remain in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} (although all other aspects of this rule apply).
 However, by using this alternate location, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} acknowledge that they are forfeiting the opportunity to use any contextual information involving the specific state of the Field at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. For example, it is impossible to appeal whether a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} was {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} or not if the Field has already been reset. If this information is pertinent to the appeal, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} should still remain in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}}, and relocate to the “question box” once the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} has been made aware of the concern and/or any relevant context.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: The Event Partner has ultimate authority regarding all non-gameplay decisions during an event
rules.description: The Event Partner has ultimate authority regarding all non-gameplay decisions during an event
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}The Event Partner has ultimate authority regarding all non-gameplay decisions during an event.{{/BOLD}} The Game Manual is intended to provide a set of rules for successfully playing V5RC Push Back; it is not intended to be an exhaustive compilation of guidelines for running a VEX V5 Robotics Competition event. Rules such as, but not limited to, the following examples are at the discretion of the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} and should be treated with the same respect as the Game Manual.
  
• Venue access
• Pit spaces
• Health and safety
• Team registration and/or competition eligibility
• Team conduct away from competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}
{{CALLOUT}} This rule exists alongside {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}}, <S1>, and <G3>. Even though there isn’t a rule that says “do not steal from the concession stand,” it would still be within an {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} authority to remove a thief from the competition.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Be prepared for minor Field variance
rules.description: Be prepared for minor Field variance
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Be prepared for minor Field variance.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} tolerances and {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} may vary from specified locations/dimensions; {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are encouraged to design their Robots accordingly. Please make sure to check Appendix A for more specific nominal dimensions and tolerances.
  
a. {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} tolerances may vary from nominal by up to ±1.0”.
b. {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} placement at the beginning of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} may vary from nominal by up to ±1” (25.4mm). If a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is within tolerance, either on the Field or within a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}, it should not be adjusted before the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} Heights may vary from nominal by up to ±1” (25.4mm).
d. {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} weight may vary by up to ± 4g
e. The rotation of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} is not specified. If a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is within tolerance, either on the Field or within a {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}}, it should not be adjusted before the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
f. Placement of {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} tape lines on the {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goals{{/LINK}} may vary from nominal by up to ± 0.25".
{{CALLOUT}} The {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} and Elements are designed to be assembled and disassembled multiple times each year. {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} store and transport {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} between events, and the individuals setting up the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} at one event may differ from those at the next. While every effort will be made to ensure minimal variance, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should expect that any {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} may be slightly different than another, and prepare accordingly. Just because something works on one {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} does not fully guarantee it will work on the next, and is not enough evidence alone to determine if a {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} is out of tolerance.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Fields may be repaired at the Event Partner’s discretion
rules.description: Fields may be repaired at the Event Partner’s discretion
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fields may be repaired at the Event Partner’s discretion.{{/BOLD}} All competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} at an event must be set up in accordance with the specifications in Appendix A and/or other applicable Sections. Minor aesthetic customizations or repairs are permitted, provided that they do not impact gameplay (see <T4>).
 Examples of permissible modifications include, but are not limited to:
  
• Applying threadlocker to {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} mounting hardware
• Using non-VEX white electrical tape to add required lines to the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}
• Using standard VEX Field tiles in place of the game-specific graphics tiles, for any reason
• Assembling {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} without nut blocks to improve alignment of holes
• Anchoring {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}} directly to {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} risers instead of the metal plates
• Anchoring the metal plates to the underlying surface with hardware or tape Examples of prohibited modifications include, but are not limited to:
  
• Unofficial {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} walls, additional structural elements inside of the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, or unofficial/replica {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}
• Additional VEX structural parts attached to a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}
• Replacing the opaque {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} walls on the VEX Portable Competition {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} with transparent panels
• Assembling a VEX Portable Competition {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} without including the securing straps
• Affixing stickers to the foam Field Tiles or otherwise marking object placements for Field reset Any specific repairs and/or modifications which pertain to the current season’s game will be documented in this rule and Appendix A, as needed.

rules.title: Fields at an event must be consistent with each other
rules.description: Fields at an event must be consistent with each other
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fields at an event must be consistent with each other.{{/BOLD}} There are many types of permissible aesthetic and/or logistical modifications that may be made to competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion. If an event has multiple Head-to-Head competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, they must all incorporate the same permissible/applicable modifications. If an event has multiple Robot Skills Challenge Fields, they must all incorporate the same permissible/applicable modifications. For example, if one Head-to-Head competition {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} is elevated, then all Head-to-Head competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} must be elevated to the same height.
 Examples of these modifications may include, but are not limited to:
  
• Elevating the playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} off of the floor (common heights are 12” to 24” [30.5cm to 61cm])
• Field control systems (see <T8>)
• Field display monitors
• {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} decorations (e.g., LED lights, sponsor decals on polycarbonate panels)
• {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} type (see <T9>)
• Utilizing the VEX GPS Field Code Strips
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Note: If an event has dedicated {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} for Skills Challenge Matches, there is no requirement for them to have the same consistent modifications as the Head-to-Head {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}. See {{LINK:_idTextAnchor161}}<T21>{{/LINK}} for more details.{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}

rules.title: There are three types of Field control that may be used
rules.description: There are three types of Field control that may be used
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T8>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}There are three types of Field control that may be used.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. A VEXnet Field Controller controlled by Tournament Manager, which connects to a Controller’s competition port via ethernet cable.
b. A V5 Event Brain controlled by Tournament Manager, which connects to a Controller via Smart Cable.
c. A VEXnet Competition Switch, which connects to a Controller’s competition port via Cat-5 cable, may only be used in {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, and Leagues, and only under extreme circumstances. If an event has multiple {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, then all {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} of the same game type must use the same control system, in accordance with <T7> and {{LINK:_idTextAnchor161}}<T21>{{/LINK}}. For example, it would be permissible for Head-to-Head competition {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} to use V5 Event Brains, and for Skills Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} to use VEXnet Field Controllers. However, it would not be permissible for one Head-to-Head Field to use a V5 Event Brain while another Head-to-Head Field uses a VEXnet Field Controller.

 Note: Official Qualifying Events may only use the official, unmodified version of Tournament Manager for field control, along with approved hardware and networking solutions found in the REC Library.
 Note 2: Add-ons that abide by the TM Public API guidelines are permitted. Once add-ons are enabled, the software is no longer supported by the REC Foundation, VEX Robotics, or DWAB Technologies; any necessary troubleshooting will be done at the user’s own risk.

rules.title: There are two types of Field Perimeter that may be used
rules.description: There are two types of Field Perimeter that may be used
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T9>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}There are two types of Field Perimeter that may be used.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. VEX Metal Competition Field Perimeter (SKU 278-1501).
b. VEX Portable Competition Field Perimeter (SKU 276-8242). See Appendix A for more details.
 If an event has multiple {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, then all {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} of the same game type must use the same {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}} type, in accordance with <T7> and {{LINK:_idTextAnchor161}}<T21>{{/LINK}}. However, it would not be permissible for one Head-to-Head Field to use a metal {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, while other Head-to-Head {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} use Portable Field Perimeters.

rules.title: Qualification Matches follow the Match schedule
rules.description: Qualification Matches follow the Match schedule
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T10>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Qualification Matches follow the Match schedule.{{/BOLD}} A Qualification {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} will be available on the day of competition. The {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} will indicate {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} partners, {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} pairings, and {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} colors for each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. For tournaments with multiple {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}, the schedule will indicate which Field each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will take place on.
  
a. {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}} may be included in the {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} at some events, but are not required. If {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}} are run, every effort will be made to equalize practice time for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}.
b. A {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} can only start before its scheduled time if all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}, Robots, and assigned volunteers are at the Field and ready to play.
c. Any multi-division event must be approved by the REC Foundation RSM prior to the event, and divisions must be assigned in sequential order by Team number.
d. The {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} is subject to change at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion.
e. If the RECF Regional Support Manager determines that a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} should be removed from further {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} at the event based on the outcome of a {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violation{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} can use the full-event {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} feature in TM to remove that {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} from all further {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} at the event. Their spots in later {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} will be replaced automatically by {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} from the end of the {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} will be removed from the end of the schedule as appropriate.

rules.title: Each Team will have at least six Qualification Matches
rules.description: Each Team will have at least six Qualification Matches

rules.title: Qualification Matches contribute to a Team’s ranking for Alliance Selection
rules.description: Qualification Matches contribute to a Team’s ranking for Alliance Selection
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T12>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Qualification Matches contribute to a Team’s ranking for Alliance Selection.{{/BOLD}}
  
a. When in a tournament, every Team will be ranked based on the same number of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}.
b. When in a league, every Team will be ranked based on the number of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} played. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that participate at least 60% of the total {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} available will be ranked above {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that participate in less than 60% of the total {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} available; e.g., if the league offers 3 ranking sessions with 4 {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} per Team, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that participate in 8 or more {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} will be ranked higher than {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who participate in 7 or fewer {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}. Being a no-show to a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} that a Team is scheduled in still constitutes participation for these calculations.
c. In some cases, a Team will be asked to play an additional {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}. The extra {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will be identified on the {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} with an asterisk; {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}} for that {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} will not impact a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} ranking, and will not affect participation percentage for leagues.

  
  i. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are reminded that {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} is always in effect and {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are expected to behave as if the additional {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} counted.
  ii. In leagues, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may have a different number of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}. Rankings are determined by the {{LINK:#winpercentage}}Win Percentage{{/LINK}}, which is the number of wins divided by the number of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} that {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} have played.

rules.title: Qualification Match tiebreakers
rules.description: Qualification Match tiebreakers
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T13>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Qualification Match tiebreakers.{{/BOLD}} Team rankings are determined throughout {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} as follows:
  
a. Average {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}} ({{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}} / number of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} played)
b. Average {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}} ({{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}} / number of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} played)
c. Average {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}} ({{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}} / number of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} played)
d. Highest {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} score
e. Second-highest {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} score
f. Random electronic draw

d. Highest {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} score
e. Second-highest {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} score
f. Random electronic draw

rules.title: Small tournaments have fewer Alliances
rules.description: Small tournaments have fewer Alliances
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T14>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Small tournaments have fewer Alliances.{{/BOLD}} The number of {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} for a given event is determined as follows, except in extraordinary circumstances with the permission of the location’s RECF Regional Support Manager:

 
{{TABLE}}
# of Teams | # of Elimination Alliances
----+----
32+ | 16
24-31 | 12
16-23 | 8
<16 | # of Teams divided by 2, less any remainder
{{/TABLE}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} This rule is applied differently for VEX U. See Rule <VUT7>.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Send a Student representative to Alliance Selection
rules.description: Send a Student representative to Alliance Selection
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T15>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Send a Student representative to Alliance Selection.{{/BOLD}} Each Team must send one (1) {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} representative to the playing Field (or other designated area) to participate in {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}. If the Team Representative fails to report in for {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}, their Team will be ineligible for participation in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} process.
 Once the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} begins, {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} representatives cannot use electronic devices unless they have been demonstrated to be in airplane mode. No electronic communication by or with {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} representatives is allowed during the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} process.
 
{{CALLOUT}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are advised to complete their scouting prior to the beginning of {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}, and to come to {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} prepared with a written list of potential {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} Partners. Non-electronic methods of communication are allowed, and rule <G2> and the {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}-Centered Policy still apply during {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}. Any communication about {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} and specific {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should be limited to {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} Members.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Each Team may only be invited once to join one Alliance
rules.description: Each Team may only be invited once to join one Alliance
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T16>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Each Team may only be invited once to join one Alliance.{{/BOLD}} If a Team representative declines an {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain’s{{/LINK}} invitation during {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}, that Team is no longer eligible to be selected by another {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}}. However, they are still eligible to play {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}} as an {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3i-Z2ucZRqM}}This video{{/LINK}} includes a full explanation of the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} process.
 For example:
  
• {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}} 1 invites Team ABC to join their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
• Team ABC declines the invitation.
• No other {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captains{{/LINK}} may invite Team ABC to join their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
• However, Team ABC may still form their own {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} if Team ABC is ranked high enough after {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} to become an {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}}.
a. {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} must have two {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}, and there are no “do-overs” during {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}. If enough {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} decline their invitations such that the full number of {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} cannot be filled, the event will proceed with a reduced number of {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}}.
b. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} must be removed after {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} due to the outcome of a {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violation{{/LINK}} (see clause e of <T10>), the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} can use the full-event {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} feature in TM to remove that {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} from their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. Their spot in that {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will be filled manually by the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} by the next-highest-ranked {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that is not already in an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. If that {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} is unavailable or unwilling to participate, the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} can manually select the next-highest-ranked and available {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}. If no replacement {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} is available, the remaining half of that {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will play alone.

rules.title: Elimination Matches follow the Elimination Bracket
rules.description: Elimination Matches follow the Elimination Bracket
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T17>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Elimination Matches follow the Elimination Bracket.{{/BOLD}} A sixteen (16) {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} bracket plays as shown in Figure T17-1, with {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} proceeding in numbered order through each round.
 If an event is run with fewer than 16 {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}}, then they will use the bracket shown in Figure T17-1, with {{LINK:#bye}}Byes{{/LINK}} awarded when there is no applicable {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. For example, in a tournament with 12 {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} 1, 2, 3, & 4 would automatically advance to the Quarterfinals.
rules.completeText:

 If an event is run with fewer than 16 {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}}, then they will use the bracket shown in Figure T17-1, with {{LINK:#bye}}Byes{{/LINK}} awarded when there is no applicable {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. For example, in a tournament with 12 {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} 1, 2, 3, & 4 would automatically advance to the Quarterfinals.

rules.title: Elimination Matches are a blend of “Best of 1” and “Best of 3
rules.description: Elimination Matches are a blend of “Best of 1” and “Best of 3
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T18>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Elimination Matches are a blend of “Best of 1” and “Best of 3.”{{/BOLD}} “Best of 1” means that the winning {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} in each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} advances to the next round of the {{LINK:#eliminationbracket}}Elimination Bracket{{/LINK}}. “Best of 3” means that the first {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} to reach two wins will advance.
 See the flowchart below for more information.
 
{{IMAGE:https://content.vexrobotics.com/docs/25-26/v5rc-push-back/online-manual/images/TournamentFlowchart.png}}
 {{BOLD}}Figure T18-1:{{/BOLD}} The process for determining how {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}} should be played.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<T18>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Elimination Matches are a blend of “Best of 1” and “Best of 3.”{{/BOLD}} “Best of 1” means that the winning {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} in each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} advances to the next round of the {{LINK:#eliminationbracket}}Elimination Bracket{{/LINK}}. “Best of 3” means that the first {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} to reach two wins will advance.
 See the flowchart below for more information.
 
{{IMAGE:https://content.vexrobotics.com/docs/25-26/v5rc-push-back/online-manual/images/TournamentFlowchart.png}}
 {{BOLD}}Figure T18-1:{{/BOLD}} The process for determining how {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}} should be played.

rules.title: Ties in Elimination Matches lead to limited rematches
rules.description: Ties in Elimination Matches lead to limited rematches
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T19>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Ties in Elimination Matches lead to limited rematches.{{/BOLD}} In the case of tied {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} during Elimination Rounds, Tournament Manager will apply the following logic to determine which {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will progress to the next round.
  
a. In a “Best of 1” Elimination Round, the higher-seeded {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will advance and be declared the winner under the following guidelines.

  
  i. After two (2) ties in a non-Finals {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  ii. After three (3) ties in a Finals {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
b. For single-division events or within a division: in a ”Best of 3” Elimination Round, the higher-seeded {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will advance and be declared the winner under the following guidelines.

  
  i. After three (3) ties in a round in which neither {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} has yet won a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (0-0).
  ii. After two (2) ties in a round in which each {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} has won a single {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (1-1).
c. For single-division events or within a division: after two (2) ties in a “Best of 3” Elimination Round in which one {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} has won a single {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (1-0), the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} with one (1) win will be declared the winner.
d. For a “Best of 3” overall Finals round at a multi-division event, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should continue to play tiebreaker {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} until one {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} has won two (2) {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Skills Match Schedule
rules.description: Skills Match Schedule
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T20>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Skills Match Schedule.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} play {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} on a first-come, first-served basis. Each Team will get the opportunity to play exactly three (3) {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Matches{{/LINK}} and three (3) {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Matches{{/LINK}} at each tournament and/or League Session.
 {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should review the event agenda and their {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} to determine when the best possible time is to complete their {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}. If the Robot Skills Challenge area closes before a Team has completed all six (6) {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, but it is determined that there was adequate time given, then the Team will automatically forfeit those unused {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.
 Details regarding logistics of Skills-Only Events can be found in the {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/5474199602071-Qualifying-Criteria-for-VIQRC-VRC-and-VEX-U-Events}}REC Foundation Qualifying Criteria{{/LINK}} document.
  
a. {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} are only available to {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who participate in {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}}, unless the event is an approved Skills-Only Event or in cases where a VURC or VAIRC team records skills scores in accordance with {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/4830586757399-Recording-VEX-U-and-or-VEX-AI-Robotics-Competition-Skills-Scores-at-VEX-V5-Robotics-Competition-Events}}this REC Library article{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who participate in {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} during a specific League Ranking Session are the only {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who can participate in {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} at that session.
b. Skills scores recorded by ineligible {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be deleted from Tournament Manager before the event is finalized on RobotEvents.com.

rules.title: There is no requirement that Skills Challenge Fields have the same consistent modifications as the Head-to-Head Fields
rules.description: There is no requirement that Skills Challenge Fields have the same consistent modifications as the Head-to-Head Fields
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T21>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}There is no requirement that Skills Challenge Fields have the same consistent modifications as the Head-to-Head Fields.{{/BOLD}} For example, there is no requirement that all Skills Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} are elevated to the same height as Head-to-Head {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}}. However, all Skills Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} at a single event must use the same type of Field control and {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, as described in rules <T8> and <T9>.
 It is strongly recommended/preferred that all Skills Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} are consistent with each other, but this may not be the case in extreme circumstances.
 In order to use non-conforming Head-to-Head {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} for Skills Challenge runs (e.g. during lunch), the following steps should be taken:
  
• {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must be informed that the Head-to-Head {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} may have some differences from the Skills Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} (e.g., they might not have GPS strips).
• {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must be given an opportunity to select which type of Field they want to use, i.e. they cannot be required to use a Head-to-Head Field for any Skills Challenge run.

However, all Skills Challenge {{LINK:#field}}Fields{{/LINK}} at a single event must use the same type of Field control and {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, as described in rules <T8> and <T9>.

• {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must be given an opportunity to select which type of Field they want to use, i.e. they cannot be required to use a Head-to-Head Field for any Skills Challenge run.

rules.title: Skills Rankings at events
rules.description: Skills Rankings at events
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T22>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Skills Rankings at events.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be ranked at an event based on the following scores and tiebreakers:
  
a. Sum of highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score and highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
b. Highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
c. Second-highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
d. Second-highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
e. Highest sum of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} (see rule <RSC4>) from a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., the {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} in point 1).
f. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} (i.e., the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in point 2).
g. Third-highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
h. Third-highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.
i. If a tie cannot be broken after all above criteria, then the following ordered criteria will be used to determine which Team had the “best” {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}:

  
  a. Points earned for filled {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zones{{/LINK}}.
  b. Number of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}}.
  c. Points earned for {{LINK:#parked}}Parking{{/LINK}}
j. If the tie still isn’t broken, the same process in Step 9 will be applied to each {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} best {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}}.
k. If the tie still isn’t broken, events may choose to allow {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} to have one more deciding {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}}, to be ranked according to the standard criteria above, or declare both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} the Robot Skills Challenge Winner.

rules.title: Skills Rankings Globally
rules.description: Skills Rankings Globally
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<T23>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Skills Rankings Globally.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be ranked globally based on their Robot Skills scores from Tournaments and Leagues that upload results to RobotEvents.com, according to the following tiebreakers:
  
a. Highest Robot Skills score (combined {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} Score from a single event).
b. Highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score (from any event).
c. Highest sum of {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Times{{/LINK}} (see rule <RSC4>) from the {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} used for point 1.
d. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} used for point 2.
e. Highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score (from any event).
f. Highest {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} from the {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score used for point 5.
g. Earliest posting of the Highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.

  
  a. The first Team to post a score ranks ahead of other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that post the same score at a later time, all else being equal.
h. Earliest posting of the Highest {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} score.

  
  a. The first Team to post a score ranks ahead of other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} that post the same score at a later time, all else being equal.
