rules.title: Robot Skills at League Events
rules.description: Robot Skills at League Events
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<T24>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robot Skills at League Events.{{/BOLD}} At league events in which {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may submit Robot Skills Challenge scores across multiple sessions, the Robot Skills scores (combined highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} scores) used for rankings will be calculated from {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} within the same session.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<T24>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Robot Skills at League Events.{{/BOLD}} At league events in which {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may submit Robot Skills Challenge scores across multiple sessions, the Robot Skills scores (combined highest {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} scores) used for rankings will be calculated from {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} within the same session.

rules.title: Different Robot placement than rule <GG10>
rules.description: Different Robot placement than rule <GG10>
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<VUG1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different Robot placement than rule <GG10>.{{/BOLD}} The red {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} has the right to place one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on the Field first, followed by both blue {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, and ending with the 2nd red {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. This applies in {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}. If this right is used, once a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} has placed a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on the Field, its position cannot be readjusted prior to the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<VUG1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different Robot placement than rule <GG10>.{{/BOLD}} The red {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} has the right to place one {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on the Field first, followed by both blue {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, and ending with the 2nd red {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. This applies in {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}. If this right is used, once a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} has placed a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} on the Field, its position cannot be readjusted prior to the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Different expansion
rules.description: Different expansion
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<VUG2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different expansion.{{/BOLD}} The designated 24” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may expand horizontally and/or vertically up to a maximum size of 24" x 24" x 24" at any time during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<VUG2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different expansion.{{/BOLD}} The designated 24” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may expand horizontally and/or vertically up to a maximum size of 24" x 24" x 24" at any time during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Different availability of Loaders
rules.description: Different availability of Loaders
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<VUG3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different availability of Loaders.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} may add {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} to the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to their {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<VUG3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different availability of Loaders.{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} may add {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} to the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to their {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Different Autonomous Win Point criteria
rules.description: Different Autonomous Win Point criteria
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUG4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different Autonomous Win Point criteria.{{/BOLD}} An {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} is awarded to any VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that ends the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} with all of the following tasks completed:
  
a. At least 12 {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color are {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}.
b. At least three (3) different {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} include at least one (1) {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color.
c. The six (6) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} Station have been removed.
d. Neither {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is partially or entirely within the vertical projection of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} or contacting the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} barrier.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUG4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different Autonomous Win Point criteria.{{/BOLD}} An {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} is awarded to any VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that ends the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} with all of the following tasks completed:
  
a. At least 12 {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color are {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}.
b. At least three (3) different {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} include at least one (1) {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color.
c. The six (6) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} Station have been removed.
d. Neither {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is partially or entirely within the vertical projection of its {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}-colored {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} or contacting the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} barrier.

rules.title: Don’t cross the Autonomous Line, and don’t interfere with your opponents’ actions
rules.description: Don’t cross the Autonomous Line, and don’t interfere with your opponents’ actions
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUG5>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Don’t cross the Autonomous Line, and don’t interfere with your opponents’ actions.{{/BOLD}} In VEX U {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}, <SG7> applies to foam tiles, {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, and interactions on the opposing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} side of the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} and on the opponent’s side of the Field beyond the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}}. Interactions outside of the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}} should still be primarily {{LINK:#offensive}}Offensive{{/LINK}}, and must meet the guidelines and limitations in rule <SG7>.
  
a. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in contact with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} or within the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}} are not considered to be on either side, and may be utilized by either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}.

Interactions outside of the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}} should still be primarily {{LINK:#offensive}}Offensive{{/LINK}}, and must meet the guidelines and limitations in rule <SG7>.
  
a. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in contact with the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} or within the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}} are not considered to be on either side, and may be utilized by either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Engage with the Autonomous Line and Neutral Zone at your own risk
rules.description: Engage with the Autonomous Line and Neutral Zone at your own risk
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUG6>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Engage with the Autonomous Line and Neutral Zone at your own risk.{{/BOLD}} This rule replaces the first paragraph of <SG8>. Any {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that engages with {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} that begin the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} or are located on the {{LINK:#autonomousline}}Autonomous Line{{/LINK}} or within the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}} should be aware that opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may also choose to do the same.
 {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} entering the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}} should expect contact with other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}, and should take this into consideration when designing and building. However, Rule <GG14> still applies within the {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} can receive a {{LINK:#disablement}}Disablement{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} for actions that a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} deems intentionally damaging or egregious.
 
{{CALLOUT}} The {{LINK:#neutralzone}}Neutral Zone{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} within it are intended to be utilized by either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. This will inevitably result in {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}-on-{{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} interactions, both incidental and intentional. The overarching intent of <VUG6> is for the vast majority of these interactions to result in no rule {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} and/or penalties for either {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}, just as no rules {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} occur in 99% of Driver Controlled interactions.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Some electronic devices may be in motion or moving at the beginning of the Match
rules.description: Some electronic devices may be in motion or moving at the beginning of the Match
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<VUG7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Some electronic devices may be in motion or moving at the beginning of the Match.{{/BOLD}} This includes active cooling fans, spinning LIDAR modules, and/or other similar sensors or {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}}. These electronic devices should not initiate any form of motion for the entire {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or any of its subsystems, and may not directly interact with game pieces and/or other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<VUG7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Some electronic devices may be in motion or moving at the beginning of the Match.{{/BOLD}} This includes active cooling fans, spinning LIDAR modules, and/or other similar sensors or {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}}. These electronic devices should not initiate any form of motion for the entire {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or any of its subsystems, and may not directly interact with game pieces and/or other {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Teams may use two (2) Robots in each Match
rules.description: Teams may use two (2) Robots in each Match
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR1>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use {{BOLD}}two (2) Robots{{/BOLD}} in each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  
a. Both {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may only be built from the following materials:

  
  i. Official VEX Robotics products (see <VUR2>)
  ii. {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}} made by the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} (see <VUR3> through <VUR7>)
  iii. Commercially-available springs, fasteners, and bearings (see <VUR8>, <VUR9>, and <VUR15>)
  iv. A legal electronics system (see <VUR10> and <VUR11>)
  v. Any legal {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} (see <VUR12>)
  vi. A legal pneumatics system (see <VUR14>)
  vii. Unmodified legal {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}} (see <VUR4> and <VUR5>)
b. One {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be no larger than 24” (609.6 mm) x 24” (609.6 mm) x 24” (609.6 mm) at the start of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
c. One {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must be no larger than 15” (381 mm) x 15” (381 mm) x 15” (381 mm) at the start of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Teams may use any official VEX Robotics products, other than the exceptions listed in the tables below, to construct their Robot
rules.description: Teams may use any official VEX Robotics products, other than the exceptions listed in the tables below, to construct their Robot
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR2>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use {{BOLD}}any official VEX Robotics products{{/BOLD}}, other than the exceptions listed in the tables below, to construct their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. This includes those from the VEXpro, VEX EXP, VEX IQ, VEX GO, VEX 123, VEX CTE, and VEX Robotics by HEXBUG* product lines. Rule <R28> applies, but most modifications to non-electrical components are allowed.
 
{{TABLE}}
SKU | Description
----+----
217-8080 | Talon SRX
217-9191 | Victor SPX
217-9090 | Victor SP
217-4243 | Pneumatic Control Module
217-4244 | Power Distribution Panel
217-4245 | Voltage Regulator Module
217-4347 | 775pro
217-2000 | CIM Motor
217-3371 | Mini CIM Motor
217-3351 | BAG Motor
217-6515 | Falcon 500
{{/TABLE}}
  This rule takes precedence over all other rules regarding {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}}, such as <VUR5>.
 {{BOLD}}{{ITALIC}}* The HEXBUG brand is a registered trademark belonging to Spin Master Corp{{/ITALIC}}
{{/BOLD}}

 
{{CALLOUT}} As of November 2025, all VEXpro parts are discontinued. To maintain a level playing field and ensure all VURC Teams have access to the same library of parts, functionally equivalent, drop-in part substitutes for VEXpro parts may be considered to meet the intent of <VUR2>.
 In order to meet our intent, each functionally equivalent, drop-in part must:
  
a. Match the form, fit, and function of the VEXpro part it is replacing.
b. Not provide a discernable or perceivable advantage over the comparable VEXpro part.
c. Comply with all other applicable VURC Robot rules. Teams should be prepared to demonstrate or defend their part substitutes in inspection as necessary, when possible.
 The GDC understands that this may be difficult in the immediate future, as the VEXpro website no longer hosts documentation on VEXpro parts, including but not limited to part descriptions, drawings, pictures, etc. The GDC is working with VEX Robotics to explore and identify a better long-term solution to this problem. In the interim, we ask all Teams, referees, inspectors, and the entire VURC community to:
  
a. Work collaboratively, understanding that the intent of this clarification is to allow Robots to compete with minimal to no modification, not to prevent participation.
b. Apply <G1>, <G3>, and the RECF Code of Conduct when interpreting this guidance. We recognize this may temporarily result in a challenging inspection environment; good faith is essential.
c. Refrain from exploiting this allowance. We intentionally and meaningfully chose to attempt to maintain product legality and competitive fairness while products are discontinued. Deliberately pushing boundaries may force the GDC to entirely reassess the legality of all VEXpro components. {{/CALLOUT}}

This includes those from the VEXpro, VEX EXP, VEX IQ, VEX GO, VEX 123, VEX CTE, and VEX Robotics by HEXBUG* product lines. rules.title: Fabricated Parts may be made by applying the following manufacturing processes to legal Raw Stock:
rules.description: Fabricated Parts may be made by applying the following manufacturing processes to legal Raw Stock:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fabricated Parts{{/BOLD}} may be made by applying the following manufacturing processes to legal {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}}:
  
a. Additive manufacturing processes, such as 3D printing.
b. Subtractive manufacturing processes, such as cutting, drilling, routing, or machining.
c. Bending, such as sheet metal braking or thermoforming.
d. Attaching materials to one another, such as welding or chemically bonding (e.g., epoxy).
e. Molding of non-metals, such as injecting polyurethane into a 3D printed mold.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR3>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fabricated Parts{{/BOLD}} may be made by applying the following manufacturing processes to legal {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}}:
  
a. Additive manufacturing processes, such as 3D printing.
b. Subtractive manufacturing processes, such as cutting, drilling, routing, or machining.
c. Bending, such as sheet metal braking or thermoforming.
d. Attaching materials to one another, such as welding or chemically bonding (e.g., epoxy).
e. Molding of non-metals, such as injecting polyurethane into a 3D printed mold.

rules.title: Fabricated Parts must be made from legal Raw Stock
rules.description: Fabricated Parts must be made from legal Raw Stock
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR4>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}} must be made from legal {{BOLD}}Raw Stock{{/BOLD}}. To be considered {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}}, the material must be obtained in one of the following forms before undergoing the fabrication processes listed in <VUR3>:
 
{{TABLE}}
 | Type | Shape / Profile | Examples
----+-----+-----+----
1 | Sheet | Flat Plane | Sheet metal
                        ⅛” polycarbonate sheet
                        Plywood
2 | Solid Billet | “Thick” rectangular beam / block | 4” x 4” x 6” solid aluminum billet
                        2” x 2” x 2” acetal block
3 | Solid Bar | “Thin” rectangular beam | 2x4 wood planks
                        ¼” x 3” aluminum bars
4 | Hollow Bar | Hollow rectangular beam | 1” x 1”, 1/32” wall aluminum box tube
5 | Solid Rod | Cylinder, Hexagonal or Rounded Hexagonal Stock | ¼” steel rod
                        ¼” acetal rod
                        VEXpro Hex Shaft
6 | Hollow Rod / Tube | Hollow Cylinder, Drilled/Threaded Hexagonal or Rounded Hexagonal Stock | Copper tubing
                        PVC pipe
                        VEXpro ThunderHex Stock
7 | Angle | 90° “L” shape | 1” x 1”, 1/16” thickness aluminum angle
8 | U- / C-Channel | “U” or “C”. See this Q&A. | 1/4” High x 1” Wide Aluminum U-Channel
9 | Non-Metal 3D Printer Filament | Thin cylinder | PLA or TPU filament
                        Composite nylon filament (e.g. Markforged OnyxTM)
10 | Synthetic Polymer used for Molding | Liquid | Polyurethane
                        Silicone
11 | Sold Sphere | Solid (not hollow) uniformly rounded stock | Steel ball bearing
                        Shaped wood finial
{{/TABLE}}
 
{{CALLOUT}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are not required to exhaustively define the specific material type for each component of every {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Part{{/LINK}} in their Engineering Notebook, as it should be obvious from the engineering drawings required by <VUR7>. However, unusual parts should be expected to receive increased scrutiny.
 If any materials do not easily fall into one of these categories, then that is probably an indication that it is not intended to be a legal type of {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}}. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} cannot demonstrate that the component was made from a legal type of {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}}, then they will be asked to remove it from their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: The following material types are not considered Raw Stock, and are therefore not permitted:
rules.description: The following material types are not considered Raw Stock, and are therefore not permitted:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR5>{{/BOLD}} The following material types are {{BOLD}}not considered Raw Stock{{/BOLD}}, and are therefore not permitted:
 
{{TABLE}}
 | Type | Examples
----+-----+----
1 | Any otherwise-legal Raw Stock that has been post-processed by drilling, machining, or otherwise removing material | Angle aluminum with regularly-spaced holes or slots
                        Perforated sheet metal
2 | Extrusions that do not fall under one of the categories listed in <VUR4> | Non-rectangular aluminum extrusions, such as 80/20, T-slot, or Octanorm
                        Gear stock
3 | Assembled items (or pre-arranged kits of unassembled items) that form a single, more complex component | Gearboxes
                        Claw mechanisms
                        Swerve drive modules
4 | Commercial Off-the-Shelf items that are intended to be used with minimal modification | Wheels
                        Gears
                        Timing belts and pulleys
5 | Materials that are intended to be cast or sintered | Resin / powdered-bed 3D printing
                        Molten aluminum used for sand casting
{{/TABLE}}
 Note: <VUR2> takes precedence over this rule. Materials purchased from VEX Robotics that fall under one of these categories (e.g., VersaFrame pre-drilled extrusion) are permitted.
 
{{CALLOUT}} In industry, terms like “raw stock,” “raw material,” and “material stock” are often used interchangeably and cover an extremely broad scope of physical goods. The lists in <VUR4> and <VUR5> are intended to explain what specific material types and profiles fall under the defined term “{{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}}” in the context of the VEX U competition.
 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Fabricated Parts cannot be made from Raw Stock which poses a safety or damage risk to the event, other Teams, or Field Elements
rules.description: Fabricated Parts cannot be made from Raw Stock which poses a safety or damage risk to the event, other Teams, or Field Elements
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR6>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}} cannot be made from {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}} which poses a {{BOLD}}safety or damage risk{{/BOLD}} to the event, other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}. Examples of prohibited materials include, but are not limited to:
  
a. Any material intended to produce flames or pyrotechnic effects.
b. Any material that is liquid at the time of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. Examples include hydraulic fluids, oils, greases, liquid mercury, and tire sealant.

  
  i. This does not include fabrication processes that involve the use of liquids, such as milling coolant or epoxy.
c. Any matter that shatters or otherwise presents an excessive Field/safety hazard upon failure. Examples include fiberglass, acrylic, and carbon fiber sheet/tube stock.

  
  i. This rule refers specifically to material legality itself. Any potentially unsafe mechanisms made from legal {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}} may still be addressed by <S1> and <R19>.
  ii. 3D printer filaments that include carbon fiber (or similar) additives or carbon fiber (or similar) inlay are exempt from this exception, and are considered legal for use in {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR6>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}} cannot be made from {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}} which poses a {{BOLD}}safety or damage risk{{/BOLD}} to the event, other {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}, or {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}. Examples of prohibited materials include, but are not limited to:
  
a. Any material intended to produce flames or pyrotechnic effects.
b. Any material that is liquid at the time of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. Examples include hydraulic fluids, oils, greases, liquid mercury, and tire sealant.

  
  i. This does not include fabrication processes that involve the use of liquids, such as milling coolant or epoxy.
c. Any matter that shatters or otherwise presents an excessive Field/safety hazard upon failure. Examples include fiberglass, acrylic, and carbon fiber sheet/tube stock.

  
  i. This rule refers specifically to material legality itself. Any potentially unsafe mechanisms made from legal {{LINK:#rawstock}}Raw Stock{{/LINK}} may still be addressed by <S1> and <R19>.
  ii. 3D printer filaments that include carbon fiber (or similar) additives or carbon fiber (or similar) inlay are exempt from this exception, and are considered legal for use in {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Fabricated Parts must be made by Team members
rules.description: Fabricated Parts must be made by Team members
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fabricated Parts must be made by Team members.{{/BOLD}} Any {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}} must be accompanied by documentation that demonstrates the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} design and construction process for that {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Part{{/LINK}}.
  
a. The minimum acceptable form of documentation is an engineering drawing with multiple views for the part in question. These drawings may be included in a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} Engineering Notebook or in a standalone appendix to the Engineering Notebook.
b. Any {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Part{{/LINK}} must have been entirely designed and produced by {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members. For example, parts ordered by the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} and 3D printed by a third party would be prohibited.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be required to provide this documentation as requested by inspectors, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}, or judges at any time at an event. Failure to provide acceptable documentation will result in the part being deemed illegal for use; therefore, <R3>, <R4>, and/or {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} will apply.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR7>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Fabricated Parts must be made by Team members.{{/BOLD}} Any {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}} must be accompanied by documentation that demonstrates the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} design and construction process for that {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Part{{/LINK}}.
  
a. The minimum acceptable form of documentation is an engineering drawing with multiple views for the part in question. These drawings may be included in a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} Engineering Notebook or in a standalone appendix to the Engineering Notebook.
b. Any {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Part{{/LINK}} must have been entirely designed and produced by {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members. For example, parts ordered by the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} and 3D printed by a third party would be prohibited.
c. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will be required to provide this documentation as requested by inspectors, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}, or judges at any time at an event. Failure to provide acceptable documentation will result in the part being deemed illegal for use; therefore, <R3>, <R4>, and/or {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}} will apply.

rules.title: Teams may use commercially-available springs on their Robots
rules.description: Teams may use commercially-available springs on their Robots
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR8>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use {{BOLD}}commercially-available springs{{/BOLD}} on their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. For the purposes of this rule, a “spring” is any device used for storing and releasing elastic potential energy. Examples include, but are not limited to:
  
a. Compression, tension, torsion, constant force, or conical springs made from spring steel.
b. Springs made from elastic thread or rubber, such as surgical tubing, bungee cords, or stretchable braided rope.
c. Closed-loop (pneumatic) gas shocks. Note: Gas shocks are not considered pneumatic devices in the context of <VUR14>. Gas shocks may not be modified in any way.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR8>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use {{BOLD}}commercially-available springs{{/BOLD}} on their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}. For the purposes of this rule, a “spring” is any device used for storing and releasing elastic potential energy. Examples include, but are not limited to:
  
a. Compression, tension, torsion, constant force, or conical springs made from spring steel.
b. Springs made from elastic thread or rubber, such as surgical tubing, bungee cords, or stretchable braided rope.
c. Closed-loop (pneumatic) gas shocks. Note: Gas shocks are not considered pneumatic devices in the context of <VUR14>. Gas shocks may not be modified in any way.

rules.title: Teams may use commercially available fastener hardware on their Robot
rules.description: Teams may use commercially available fastener hardware on their Robot
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR9>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may use {{BOLD}}commercially available fastener hardware{{/BOLD}} on their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. Examples include (but are not limited to):
  
• Screws, nuts, rivets, and heatset inserts.
• Hinges, pins, rod ends, threaded rods, and hose clamps.
• Ancillary fastener accessories, such as washers or spacers.
• Adhesives such as epoxy, glue, or tape (only when used to join together two parts). If the primary function of the part is not “fastening”, then <VUR5>, <VUR6>, and/or <VUR7> take precedence over this rule. Illegal examples include (but are not limited to):
  
• A prefabricated non-VEX wheel, even though it may technically connect tread to a shaft
• 80/20 extrusion; other items get “fastened to it”, it is not the part doing the “fastening”
• Using grip tape to improve wheel traction
rules.completeText:

rules.title: Each Robot must utilize exactly one (1) V5 Robot Brain and at least one (1) V5 Robot Radio connected to a V5 Controller
rules.description: Each Robot must utilize exactly one (1) V5 Robot Brain and at least one (1) V5 Robot Radio connected to a V5 Controller
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR10>{{/BOLD}} Each {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must utilize exactly {{BOLD}}one (1) V5 Robot Brain and at least one (1) V5 Robot Radio{{/BOLD}} connected to a V5 Controller.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must abide by the power rules noted in <R13> and <VUR12d>.
b. Wireless communication between {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} is permitted if using legal V5 Robot Brains and V5 Robot Radios. No other types of wireless communication protocols (e.g., radio, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi) are permitted.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR10>{{/BOLD}} Each {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must utilize exactly {{BOLD}}one (1) V5 Robot Brain and at least one (1) V5 Robot Radio{{/BOLD}} connected to a V5 Controller.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} must abide by the power rules noted in <R13> and <VUR12d>.
b. Wireless communication between {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} is permitted if using legal V5 Robot Brains and V5 Robot Radios. No other types of wireless communication protocols (e.g., radio, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi) are permitted.

rules.title: There is no restriction on the number of V5 Smart Motors (11W) [276-4840] and/or Smart Motors (5
rules.description: There is no restriction on the number of V5 Smart Motors (11W) [276-4840] and/or Smart Motors (5
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR11>{{/BOLD}} There is {{BOLD}}no restriction on the number of V5 Smart Motors (11W) [276-4840] and/or Smart Motors (5.5W) [276-4842]{{/BOLD}} that {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use. No other motors, servos, or electronic actuators are permitted, including those sold by VEX (e.g., the 2-Wire 393 Motor).
 Note 1: Rule <R28> still applies in VEX U. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not modify Smart Motors, and must use official/unmodified gear cartridges.
 Note 2: Commercially available pneumatic actuators and pneumatic solenoids are permitted within the guidelines of <VUR14>.
 Note 3: Legal {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} may include their own motor, servo, or actuator, per <VUR12>.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR11>{{/BOLD}} There is {{BOLD}}no restriction on the number of V5 Smart Motors (11W) [276-4840] and/or Smart Motors (5.5W) [276-4842]{{/BOLD}} that {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may use. No other motors, servos, or electronic actuators are permitted, including those sold by VEX (e.g., the 2-Wire 393 Motor).
 Note 1: Rule <R28> still applies in VEX U. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not modify Smart Motors, and must use official/unmodified gear cartridges.
 Note 2: Commercially available pneumatic actuators and pneumatic solenoids are permitted within the guidelines of <VUR14>.
 Note 3: Legal {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} may include their own motor, servo, or actuator, per <VUR12>.

rules.title: There is no restriction on commercially available Sensors, External Processors, or Additional Electronics that Robots may use for sensing and processing, except as follows:
rules.description: There is no restriction on commercially available Sensors, External Processors, or Additional Electronics that Robots may use for sensing and processing, except as follows:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR12>{{/BOLD}} There is {{BOLD}}no restriction on commercially available Sensors, External Processors, or Additional Electronics{{/BOLD}} that Robots may use for sensing and processing, except as follows:
  
a. {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processors{{/LINK}} MUST be connected to the V5 Robot Brain via any of the externally accessible ports (i.e., without any modification to the commercially available electronics). A {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}} may be connected to an {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processor{{/LINK}} which then connects to the V5 Robot Brain.
b. {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processors{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} CANNOT directly electrically interface with VEX motors and / or solenoid.
c. {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} may only receive power from any of the following:

  
  i. Directly from the V5 Robot Brain via any externally accessible port.
  ii. From an additional lithium ion, lithium iron, or nickel metal hydride battery pack (only one (1) additional battery can be used for {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}}/processing power). This additional battery pack must operate at a maximum of less than 13 volts.
  iii. Directly from an {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processor{{/LINK}}
d. Only the V5 Battery can power the V5 Brain.
e. {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processors{{/LINK}}, and/or {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} which include a low-powered motor as an integral part of their primary sensing/processing function, such as an {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processor’s{{/LINK}} cooling fan or a spinning {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}}, are permissible.

  
  i. Standalone motors which serve no additional sensing or processing functionality (e.g., using a commercially-available brushless motor in a drivetrain) are not considered legal {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}}, and would be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <VUR11>.
f. Pneumatic solenoids are the only types of solenoids that are permitted as {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}}. Solenoids used for any purpose other than opening and closing a pneumatic valve are considered an actuator and therefore prohibited, per <VUR11>.
g. <R28> still applies in VEX U, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may not alter or modify electronic parts from the VEX product lines. It is legal to rotate the blocks of the V5 Optical Sensor and V5 Distance Sensor relative to their bases, and to manufacture custom back plates for these sensors.
rules.completeText:

a. {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processors{{/LINK}} MUST be connected to the V5 Robot Brain via any of the externally accessible ports (i.e., without any modification to the commercially available electronics). A {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}} may be connected to an {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processor{{/LINK}} which then connects to the V5 Robot Brain.
b. {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processors{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} CANNOT directly electrically interface with VEX motors and / or solenoid.

  ii. From an additional lithium ion, lithium iron, or nickel metal hydride battery pack (only one (1) additional battery can be used for {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}}/processing power). This additional battery pack must operate at a maximum of less than 13 volts.
  iii. Directly from an {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processor{{/LINK}}
d. Only the V5 Battery can power the V5 Brain.
e. {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processors{{/LINK}}, and/or {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} which include a low-powered motor as an integral part of their primary sensing/processing function, such as an {{LINK:#externalprocessors}}External Processor’s{{/LINK}} cooling fan or a spinning {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}}, are permissible.

Standalone motors which serve no additional sensing or processing functionality (e.g., using a commercially-available brushless motor in a drivetrain) are not considered legal {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}}, and would be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of <VUR11>.

rules.title: Commercially available Electromechanical Assemblies are not legal for use on Robots
rules.description: Commercially available Electromechanical Assemblies are not legal for use on Robots
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR13>{{/BOLD}} Commercially available {{BOLD}}Electromechanical Assemblies are not legal{{/BOLD}} for use on {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
  
a. For the purposes of this rule, any system that integrates {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}} with other mechanical parts that are fabricated by anyone other than {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members and which serve more use than the basic definition of a {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}} would be considered an {{LINK:#electromechanicalassembly}}Electromechanical Assembly{{/LINK}}, and is therefore not legal.
b. Examples may include but are not limited to: odometry pods.
c. Commercially available {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}} with simple plastic housings that do not have any use beyond protecting internal components and aiding in mounting of the {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}} are not considered Electromechanical Assemblies.
{{CALLOUT}} The intent of this rule is to remind teams to focus their efforts on integrating custom parts with the VEX Robotics ecosystem. The VEX U Competition operates within a semi-closed system, not an open-build system. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should make efforts to use VEX Robotics parts where possible. Parts like additional sensors (LIDAR, encoders, etc.) should generally be considered okay, but assemblies/systems from other robotics suppliers that remove the challenge of systems integration should not be considered a legal part.
 {{/CALLOUT}}
rules.completeText:

 {{/CALLOUT}}

rules.title: Teams may utilize an unlimited amount of the following commercially available pneumatic components: cylinders, actuators, valves, gauges, storage tanks, regulators, manifolds, tubing, and solenoids
rules.description: Teams may utilize an unlimited amount of the following commercially available pneumatic components: cylinders, actuators, valves, gauges, storage tanks, regulators, manifolds, tubing, and solenoids
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR14>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may utilize an {{BOLD}}unlimited amount of the following commercially available pneumatic components:{{/BOLD}} cylinders, actuators, valves, gauges, storage tanks, regulators, manifolds, tubing, and solenoids.
  
a. Pneumatic devices may only be charged to a maximum of 100 psi
b. Compressors or any other forms of “on-Robot” charging are not permitted
c. All commercial components must be rated for 100 psi or higher. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should be prepared to provide documentation that verifies these ratings to inspectors if requested
d. Components must not be modified from their original state, other than the following exceptions:

  
  i. Cutting pneumatic tubing or wiring to length; assembling components using pre-existing threads, brackets, or fittings; or minor cosmetic labels
e. If commercially available 12V solenoids are used, these are considered {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} and must therefore satisfy all conditions listed in <VUR12>. 12V solenoids may be either powered by an additional power source (per <VUR12d>), or by a 5V-12V step-up converter from the V5 Robot Brain. If an external power source (or other {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} device) is used to interface with the solenoid, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} MUST be able to demonstrate that there is no way for the solenoid to receive power while the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is receiving a {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabled{{/LINK}} state from the field controller
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR14>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may utilize an {{BOLD}}unlimited amount of the following commercially available pneumatic components:{{/BOLD}} cylinders, actuators, valves, gauges, storage tanks, regulators, manifolds, tubing, and solenoids.
  
a. Pneumatic devices may only be charged to a maximum of 100 psi
b. Compressors or any other forms of “on-Robot” charging are not permitted
c. All commercial components must be rated for 100 psi or higher. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} should be prepared to provide documentation that verifies these ratings to inspectors if requested
d. Components must not be modified from their original state, other than the following exceptions:

  
  i. Cutting pneumatic tubing or wiring to length; assembling components using pre-existing threads, brackets, or fittings; or minor cosmetic labels
e. If commercially available 12V solenoids are used, these are considered {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} and must therefore satisfy all conditions listed in <VUR12>. 12V solenoids may be either powered by an additional power source (per <VUR12d>), or by a 5V-12V step-up converter from the V5 Robot Brain. If an external power source (or other {{LINK:#additionalelectronics}}Additional Electronics{{/LINK}} device) is used to interface with the solenoid, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} MUST be able to demonstrate that there is no way for the solenoid to receive power while the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is receiving a {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabled{{/LINK}} state from the field controller

rules.title: Teams may use commercially available bearings on their Robot
rules.description: Teams may use commercially available bearings on their Robot
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR15>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Teams may use commercially available bearings on their Robot.{{/BOLD}} For the purpose of this rule, a ‘bearing’ is a part that supports external loads, reduces friction, and improves efficiency by facilitating smooth dynamic motion between components. Legal examples include (but are not limited to):
  
• Parts supporting rotational motion: radial bearings, roller bearings, thrust bearings, needle bearings, one-way bearings, bushings, etc.
• Parts supporting linear motion: linear bearings, linear slides, drawer slides, etc.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUR15>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Teams may use commercially available bearings on their Robot.{{/BOLD}} For the purpose of this rule, a ‘bearing’ is a part that supports external loads, reduces friction, and improves efficiency by facilitating smooth dynamic motion between components. Legal examples include (but are not limited to):
  
• Parts supporting rotational motion: radial bearings, roller bearings, thrust bearings, needle bearings, one-way bearings, bushings, etc.
• Parts supporting linear motion: linear bearings, linear slides, drawer slides, etc.

rules.title: Instead of a 2-Team Alliance format, VURC Head-to-Head Matches will be played 1-Team vs
rules.description: Instead of a 2-Team Alliance format, VURC Head-to-Head Matches will be played 1-Team vs
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUT1>{{/BOLD}} Instead of a 2-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} format, VURC Head-to-Head {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} will be played 1-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} vs. 1-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will use two (2) {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} in each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are allowed to build and bring as many {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} as they would like, but only two (2)—one of each size as described in <VUR1>—may be brought from the pit to the playing Field for any {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
b. All {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must pass inspection before they are allowed to compete.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUT1>{{/BOLD}} Instead of a 2-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} format, VURC Head-to-Head {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} will be played 1-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} vs. 1-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will use two (2) {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} in each {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  
a. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are allowed to build and bring as many {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} as they would like, but only two (2)—one of each size as described in <VUR1>—may be brought from the pit to the playing Field for any {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
b. All {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} must pass inspection before they are allowed to compete.

rules.title: The Autonomous Period at the beginning of each Head-to-Head Match will be 30 seconds
rules.description: The Autonomous Period at the beginning of each Head-to-Head Match will be 30 seconds
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUT4>{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} at the beginning of each Head-to-Head {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} will be 30 seconds.
  
a. Human interaction with {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} is strictly prohibited.
b. If both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} complete their routines before 30 seconds have elapsed, they have the option to signal that they wish to end the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} early. Both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} must all agree on the “early stop.” This is not a requirement, and the option must have been established for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} at the event, such as during the event meeting.
rules.completeText:

b. If both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} complete their routines before 30 seconds have elapsed, they have the option to signal that they wish to end the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} early. Both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} must all agree on the “early stop.” This is not a requirement, and the option must have been established for all {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} at the event, such as during the event meeting.

rules.description: The Driver Controlled Period is shortened to 90 seconds and immediately follows the Autonomous Period

rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<VUT5>{{/BOLD}} The {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} is shortened to 90 seconds and immediately follows the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: VEX U Student eligibility
rules.description: VEX U Student eligibility
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUT6>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#vexustudent}}VEX U Student{{/LINK}} eligibility.
  
a. All VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members MUST be matriculated in a post-secondary school OR have earned a post-secondary education diploma, certificate, or other equivalent during the six (6) months preceding the VEX Robotics World Championship. The intent of this rule is to permit {{LINK:#vexustudent}}VEX U Students{{/LINK}} graduating mid-year to still be able to finish their competition season.
b. Professionals not enrolled in post-secondary education are not eligible to participate on a VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} who are dual-enrolled in both a secondary school and in post-secondary courses are not eligible to participate on a VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
d. {{LINK:#vexustudent}}VEX U Students{{/LINK}} may only be on exactly one (1) VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for the season. See <G5>.
e. If at least one member of a VEX U Robotics {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is aged 18 or older, rule <S2> does not apply.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VUT6>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#vexustudent}}VEX U Student{{/LINK}} eligibility.
  
a. All VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members MUST be matriculated in a post-secondary school OR have earned a post-secondary education diploma, certificate, or other equivalent during the six (6) months preceding the VEX Robotics World Championship. The intent of this rule is to permit {{LINK:#vexustudent}}VEX U Students{{/LINK}} graduating mid-year to still be able to finish their competition season.
b. Professionals not enrolled in post-secondary education are not eligible to participate on a VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
c. {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} who are dual-enrolled in both a secondary school and in post-secondary courses are not eligible to participate on a VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
d. {{LINK:#vexustudent}}VEX U Students{{/LINK}} may only be on exactly one (1) VEX U {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for the season. See <G5>.
e. If at least one member of a VEX U Robotics {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is aged 18 or older, rule <S2> does not apply.

rules.title: VURC tournaments have fewer Teams in Eliminations
rules.description: VURC tournaments have fewer Teams in Eliminations
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VUT7>{{/BOLD}} VURC tournaments have fewer {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in Eliminations. The number of {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in Eliminations for a given event is determined as follows, except in extraordinary circumstances with the permission of the RECF Regional Support Manager. A number of {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} below 16 will result in one or more {{LINK:#bye}}Byes{{/LINK}} for highest-ranking {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}.
 
{{TABLE}}
# of Teams | # of Elimination Teams
----+----
16+ | 16
<16 | # of Teams
{{/TABLE}}

rules.title: VEX U Robot Skills Matches use the same Field layout, object placements, and tape lines as VEX U Head-to-Head Matches
rules.description: VEX U Robot Skills Matches use the same Field layout, object placements, and tape lines as VEX U Head-to-Head Matches
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VURS1>{{/BOLD}} VEX U {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} use the same Field layout, object placements, and tape lines as VEX U Head-to-Head {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.
  
a. If a VURC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} runs {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} at a V5RC event, they may choose from the following options:

  
  i. Choose to run their {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} on a Field with the V5RC tape lines.
  ii. Work with the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} to replace the tape lines on a Skills Field to match the VURC tape layout. This option may not be available at all events, and the VURC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} should communicate with the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} in advance to discuss options. The VURC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} might need to provide their own tape.
rules.completeText:

a. If a VURC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} runs {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}} at a V5RC event, they may choose from the following options:

This option may not be available at all events, and the VURC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} should communicate with the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}} in advance to discuss options. The VURC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} might need to provide their own tape.

rules.title: A Team receives a Control Bonus if the outermost Block Scored in one end of a Long Goal and the nearest corresponding end of a Center Goal are the same color as the Team
rules.description: A Team receives a Control Bonus if the outermost Block Scored in one end of a Long Goal and the nearest corresponding end of a Center Goal are the same color as the Team
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAISC1>{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} receives a {{BOLD}}Control Bonus{{/BOLD}} if the outermost {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in one end of a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} and the nearest corresponding end of a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}} are the same color as the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
  
a. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} must be considered {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} (see <SC2>) to count toward a {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonus{{/LINK}}.
b. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} that is entirely within the space defined by the outer edges of the two white tape lines of a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} does not count toward the {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonus{{/LINK}} (see figure VAISC1-1).
c. In a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} that is closest to that end of the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} will count toward the {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonus{{/LINK}} (see figure VAISC1-2). If only one {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}}, it will count toward both of the {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonuses{{/LINK}} which include that {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VAISC1>{{/BOLD}} A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} receives a {{BOLD}}Control Bonus{{/BOLD}} if the outermost {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in one end of a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} and the nearest corresponding end of a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}} are the same color as the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
  
a. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} must be considered {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} (see <SC2>) to count toward a {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonus{{/LINK}}.
b. A {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} that is entirely within the space defined by the outer edges of the two white tape lines of a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} does not count toward the {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonus{{/LINK}} (see figure VAISC1-1).
c. In a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} that is closest to that end of the {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} will count toward the {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonus{{/LINK}} (see figure VAISC1-2). If only one {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}}, it will count toward both of the {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonuses{{/LINK}} which include that {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: A VAIRC Robot is considered Parked at the end of the Match if it meets all of the following criteria:
rules.description: A VAIRC Robot is considered Parked at the end of the Match if it meets all of the following criteria:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAISC2>{{/BOLD}} A VAIRC {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is considered {{BOLD}}Parked{{/BOLD}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} if it meets all of the following criteria:
  
a. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is designated as the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} 24” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for the current {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
b. The {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} meets all criteria of rule <SC4>.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VAISC2>{{/BOLD}} A VAIRC {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is considered {{BOLD}}Parked{{/BOLD}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} if it meets all of the following criteria:

rules.title: Scoring of the Isolation Bonus is evaluated immediately after the Isolation Period ends (i
rules.description: Scoring of the Isolation Bonus is evaluated immediately after the Isolation Period ends (i
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAISC3>{{/BOLD}} Scoring of the {{BOLD}}Isolation Bonus{{/BOLD}} is evaluated immediately after the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} ends (i.e., once all {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Elements{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} on the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} come to rest).
  
a. Points for {{LINK:#parked}}Parked{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} are not included in the calculation of a {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} score for the purposes of determining the {{LINK:#isolationbonus}}Isolation Bonus{{/LINK}}.
b. If the {{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}} ends in a tie, including a zero-to-zero tie, each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will receive an {{LINK:#isolationbonus}}Isolation Bonus{{/LINK}} of five (5) points.
c. Any {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}}, Major or Minor, committed during the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} will result in the {{LINK:#isolationbonus}}Isolation Bonus{{/LINK}} being automatically awarded to the opposing {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. See <GG13>.
d. Per rule <GG13>, if both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} commit {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}} that would have affected the outcome of the {{LINK:#isolationbonus}}Isolation Bonus{{/LINK}}, then no {{LINK:#isolationbonus}}Isolation Bonus{{/LINK}} will be awarded.

rules.title: Isolation Win Point criteria
rules.description: Isolation Win Point criteria
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAISC4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Isolation Win Point criteria.{{/BOLD}} An {{LINK:#isolationwinpoint}}Isolation Win Point{{/LINK}} is awarded to any VEX AI {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that ends the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} with all of the following tasks completed:
  
i. At least nine (9) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} color are {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}.
ii. At least three (3) different {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} include at least one {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} (1) {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} color.
iii. Neither {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is contacting the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} barrier.
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VAISC4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Isolation Win Point criteria.{{/BOLD}} An {{LINK:#isolationwinpoint}}Isolation Win Point{{/LINK}} is awarded to any VEX AI {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that ends the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} with all of the following tasks completed:
  
i. At least nine (9) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} color are {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}.
ii. At least three (3) different {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}} include at least one {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} (1) {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} of the {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} color.
iii. Neither {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is contacting the {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} barrier.

rules.title: Standard game rules apply
rules.description: Standard game rules apply
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIG1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Standard game rules apply.{{/BOLD}} All <Gx> and <Sx> rules apply as written. All other rules apply as written, except for those V5 rules that are modified below or by VEX U rules.
 Note: All references assume that the terms “{{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}” and “{{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}” are replaced with “{{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}}” and “{{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}}”, respectively.

rules.title: Autonomous means “no humans
rules.description: Autonomous means “no humans
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIG2>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Autonomous means “no humans.”{{/BOLD}} As noted by <GG12>, {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} are not permitted to interact with their {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} in any way while they are operating autonomously (i.e., during the entirety of a VAIRC {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}). The following exceptions are permitted:
  
a. Using a V5 Controller to disable a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} which is engaging in reckless or unsafe behavior, with the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} permission. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} which are {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabled{{/LINK}} may not be re-enabled for the rest of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Teams are responsible for the actions of their Robots
rules.description: Teams are responsible for the actions of their Robots

rules.title: Different expansion
rules.description: Different expansion
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIG4>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Different expansion.{{/BOLD}} In addition to the expanded sizes described in rules <SG2> and <VUG2>, each VAIRC {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may also extend up to 3’ vertically during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} to provide expanded visual perspectives for {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} within the following conditions:
  
a. Any extensions or combinations of extensions above 22” for the 15” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or above 24” for the 24” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} must fit within a vertical cylinder 2” in diameter, with the exception that one camera or vision sensor per {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} may be attached to the top of the vertical extension.
b. This expanded vertical extension cannot be used to manipulate, lift, contain, or move {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}.
c. This vertical extension rotates with the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, and it should not be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} if an extended {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} accidentally tips over during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:

b. This expanded vertical extension cannot be used to manipulate, lift, contain, or move {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}.
c. This vertical extension rotates with the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, and it should not be considered a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} if an extended {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} accidentally tips over during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: Blocks that leave the Field during a VAIRC Robot Skills Match must remain outside the Field for the remainder of the Match, and cannot be reintroduced
rules.description: Blocks that leave the Field during a VAIRC Robot Skills Match must remain outside the Field for the remainder of the Match, and cannot be reintroduced
rules.fullText: {{BOLD}}<VAIRS6>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that leave the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during a VAIRC {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} must remain outside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} for the remainder of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and cannot be reintroduced.
rules.completeText: {{BOLD}}<VAIRS6>{{/BOLD}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that leave the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during a VAIRC {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} must remain outside the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} for the remainder of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and cannot be reintroduced.

rules.title: Scoring VAIRC Robot Skills Matches
rules.description: Scoring VAIRC Robot Skills Matches
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIRS7>{{/BOLD}} Scoring VAIRC {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}. For each VAIRC {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} are awarded a score based on the following rules and scoring table:
  
a. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will receive points for one color of {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} per {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}, determined by which color represents the majority of {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in that {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (e.g., if there are 7 red {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} and 5 blue {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will only receive points for the red {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}). If an equal number of red and blue {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}, none of those {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} will receive points.
b. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will receive points for each {{LINK:#bullseye}}Bullseye{{/LINK}} with one or more {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} at least partially within its vertical projection at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. (See Figure VAIRS7-1.) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that are deemed "too close to call" by the referee should be counted as being within the vertical projection.
c. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will receive points for each blue {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} that ends the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in one of the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to the blue {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}, and for each red {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} that ends the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} in one of the {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} adjacent to the red {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} (up to six per Loader).

  
  i. A Loader that ends the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} containing six {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that match the color of the adjacent {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} will receive additional points.
d. The {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} will earn points for a Parked {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} as follows:

  
  i. A 24” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can only use the red {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}, and must meet all criteria of rule <SC4> at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  ii. A 15” {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can only use the blue {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}}, and must meet all criteria of rule <SC4> at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
e. {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zones{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonuses{{/LINK}} are not considered in VAIRC {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.

e. {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zones{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#controlbonus}}Control Bonuses{{/LINK}} are not considered in VAIRC {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: The following VURC rules apply as written:
rules.description: The following VURC rules apply as written:
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIT1>{{/BOLD}} The following VURC rules apply as written:
  
• <VUT1> - {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} are played 1-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} vs 1-{{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, with two {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} each.
• <VUT2> - {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} are a 1v1 version of a standard V5RC tournament.
• <VUT3> - {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}} are a 1v1 version of a standard V5RC tournament.
• <VUT7> - Numbers of {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} in {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}. The following VURC rules apply, replacing the terms “{{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}” and “{{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}” with “{{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}}” and “{{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}}”, respectively:
  
• <VUT4> - The {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} is 15 seconds. The option to end the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} early is available by default if both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} agree, and does not need to be established during the event meeting.
• <VUT5> - The {{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}} is one minute 45 seconds. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} have the option to signal that they wish to end the {{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}} early. Both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} must all agree on the “early stop.” This is not a requirement, and the option is available in all {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} by default.

rules.title: VEX AI Robotics Competition Teams may consist of Students that fall into one of the following categories
rules.description: VEX AI Robotics Competition Teams may consist of Students that fall into one of the following categories
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIT2>{{/BOLD}} VEX AI Robotics Competition {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may consist of {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} that fall into one of the following categories. This rule takes precedence over rule <VUT6>.
  
a. High School {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}, as described by the definition of {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}. This includes Middle School {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} who are “playing up” and competing as High School {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}.
b. Post-secondary {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}, as defined by rule <VUT6>. Note: The same {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may not consist of {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} that fall into both categories (i.e., a blended High School and University {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}).
rules.completeText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIT2>{{/BOLD}} VEX AI Robotics Competition {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may consist of {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} that fall into one of the following categories. This rule takes precedence over rule <VUT6>.
  
a. High School {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}, as described by the definition of {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}. This includes Middle School {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} who are “playing up” and competing as High School {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}.
b. Post-secondary {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}, as defined by rule <VUT6>. Note: The same {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may not consist of {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} that fall into both categories (i.e., a blended High School and University {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}).

rules.title: Each Student may only participate on one (1) VAIRC Team in a given season
rules.description: Each Student may only participate on one (1) VAIRC Team in a given season
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIT3>{{/BOLD}} Each {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} may only participate on one (1) VAIRC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} in a given season. However, a VAIRC {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} can also be a member of a V5RC or VURC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} in the same season.
 Note: See <VAIRM3> for more details regarding how this rule pertains to {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.
rules.completeText:

 Note: See <VAIRM3> for more details regarding how this rule pertains to {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}}.

rules.title: VAIRC Teams may also participate in the V5RC or VURC programs
rules.description: VAIRC Teams may also participate in the V5RC or VURC programs
rules.fullText:
{{BOLD}}<VAIRM3>{{/BOLD}} VAIRC {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may also participate in the V5RC or VURC programs. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} used in those programs can be used as-is or modified for use in the VAIRC program provided they pass all VAIRC inspection rules, and the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} on the VAIRC {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} are the same as on the V5RC or VURC counterpart {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
 The intent of this rule is to allow {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} who compete in both programs to potentially utilize the same {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} and/or subsystems. In other words, <R1> does not “cross programs.”
 {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} participating in both programs should be aware that this does not grant any exemption from any rules that might differ between programs. For example, the motor limit that is removed in VAIRC still applies for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} participating in a V5RC event.
rules.completeText:
