 {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} participating in both programs should be aware that this does not grant any exemption from any rules that might differ between programs. For example, the motor limit that is removed in VAIRC still applies for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} participating in a V5RC event.

generalDefinitions.definition: {{BOLD}}Adult{{/BOLD}} - Anyone who is not a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} or another defined term (e.g., {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}).
{{BOLD}}Alliance{{/BOLD}} - A pre-assigned grouping of two (2) {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} that are paired together during a given {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Alliance Captain{{/BOLD}} - One of the {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} with the privilege of inviting another available Team to form an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}. See <T16>.

{{BOLD}}Alliance Selection{{/BOLD}} - The process of choosing the permanent {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} for the {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}} proceeds as follows:

  
a. The highest-ranked Team at the end of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} becomes the first {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}}.
b. The {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}} invites another Team to join their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}.
c. The invited Team representative either accepts or declines as outlined in <T16>.
d. The next-highest-ranked Team becomes the next {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captain{{/LINK}}.
e. {{LINK:#alliancecaptain}}Alliance Captains{{/LINK}} continue to select their {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} in this order until all {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} are formed for the {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Alliance Station{{/BOLD}} - The designated regions where the {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} must remain for the duration of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Autonomous Bonus{{/BOLD}} - A point bonus awarded to the {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} that has earned the most points at the end of the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. See <SC5> for more information.

{{BOLD}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/BOLD}} - An {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} consists of a sixty-second )(one minute) {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. There is no {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} can elect to end their run early if they wish to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Autonomous Period{{/BOLD}} - A time period during which {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} operate and react only to sensor inputs and pre-programmed commands.

{{BOLD}}Autonomous Points (AP){{/BOLD}} - The second basis of ranking {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}. An {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} who wins the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} earns ten (10) {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}. In the event of a tie, both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} will receive five (5) {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Autonomous Win Point (AWP){{/BOLD}} - An additional Win Point awarded to any {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} that has completed a defined set of tasks at the end of the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} of a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}. Both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} can earn an {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} if both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} accomplish these tasks. See <SC6> for more information.
{{BOLD}}Bye{{/BOLD}} - A situation in which an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} automatically advances to the next round of tournament play without competing.

{{BOLD}}Disablement{{/BOLD}} - A penalty applied to a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for a safety {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that receives a {{LINK:#disablement}}Disablement{{/LINK}} is not allowed to operate their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} for the remainder of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and the {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}}(s) will be asked to place their controller(s) on the ground or another safe location outside of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, as directed by the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Disqualification{{/BOLD}} - A penalty applied to a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}. A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that receives a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} receives zero (0) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}, (0) {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Points{{/LINK}}, (0) {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}, and (0) {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}}. When a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} receives a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} in an {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Match{{/LINK}}, the entire {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} and they receive a loss for the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. At the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion, repeated {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualifications{{/LINK}} for a single {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may lead to its {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} for the entire tournament (see <GG6>). A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that receives a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}} receives a score of zero (0) for that {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Drive Team Member{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} who stands in the {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Station{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are not allowed to be {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}}. See rule <GG1>.
{{BOLD}}Driver Controlled Period{{/BOLD}} - A time period during which {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} operate their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} via remote control.

{{BOLD}}Driving Skills Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} consists of a sixty-second (one minute) {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}}. There is no {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} can elect to end their run early if they wish to record a {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Electromechanical Assembly{{/BOLD}} - A complex system composed of multiple off-the-shelf components, which may include {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensors{{/LINK}}, mechanical parts, and actuators.
{{BOLD}}Elimination Bracket{{/BOLD}} - A schedule of {{LINK:#eliminationmatch}}Elimination Matches{{/LINK}} for eight (8) to sixteen (16) {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}}. See <T17>.

{{BOLD}}Elimination Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} used in the process of determining the champion {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} of two (2) {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} face off according to the {{LINK:#eliminationbracket}}Elimination Bracket{{/LINK}}; the winning {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} moves on to the next round.

{{BOLD}}Entanglement{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} status. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#entangle}}Entangled{{/LINK}} if it has grabbed, hooked, or attached to an opposing {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} or a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}. See rule <GG14>.

{{BOLD}}Event Partner{{/BOLD}} - The volunteer VEX V5 Robotics Competition tournament coordinator who serves as an overall manager for the volunteers, venue, event materials, and all other event considerations. {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partners{{/LINK}} serve as the official liaison between the REC Foundation, the event volunteers, and event attendees.
{{BOLD}}Field{{/BOLD}} - The entire playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, comprising of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} and the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Field Element{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, white tape, {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#goal}}Goals{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zones{{/LINK}}, and all supporting structures and accessories (such as field monitors, etc.).
{{BOLD}}Field Perimeter{{/BOLD}} - The outer part of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, made up of 12 straight sections.

{{BOLD}}Floor{{/BOLD}} - The interior flat part of the playing {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, made up of an array of six (6) gray foam field tiles wide by six (6) gray foam field tiles long (totaling 36 field tiles) that are within the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Goalkeeping{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} status and/or {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} action. In Push Back, a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} while it is reaching into any open portion of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} while not attempting to change the score of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} through legal means (i.e., the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not actively moving {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} inside a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}, and is not adding or removing {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} to/from a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} or a {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}}), or while it is simultaneously reaching into both open portions of a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}}. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} can be in possession of a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} and capable of scoring, but still be {{LINK:#goalkeeping}}Goalkeeping{{/LINK}} based on its actions. See <SG10> for more information.

{{BOLD}}Head Referee{{/BOLD}} - A certified impartial volunteer responsible for enforcing the rules in this manual as written. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} are the only individuals who may discuss ruling interpretations or scoring questions with {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} at an event. Large events (e.g., Signature Events, World Championships, etc.) might include multiple {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion.

{{BOLD}}Holding{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} status; see rule <GG17> for more information. {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} is legal until it exceeds the limits in <GG17>. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is considered to be {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} if it meets any of the following criteria during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}:
 
• {{BOLD}}Trapping{{/BOLD}} - Limiting the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to a small or confined area of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, approximately the size of one foam field tile or less, without an avenue for escape. Note that if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not attempting to escape, it is not considered {{LINK:#trap}}Trapped{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Pinning{{/BOLD}} - Preventing the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} through contact with the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}, or another {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Lifting{{/BOLD}} - Controlling an opponent’s movements by raising or tilting the opponent’s {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} off of the foam tiles. Preventing a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that is already off of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} from returning to the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} may also be considered {{LINK:#lift}}Lifting{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#trap}}Trapping{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Match{{/BOLD}} - A set time period, consisting of a {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} and/or {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Periods{{/LINK}}, during which {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} play a defined version of Push Back to earn points.
  
• {{BOLD}}Autonomous Period{{/BOLD}} - A time period during which {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} operate and react only to sensor inputs and pre-programmed commands.
• {{BOLD}}Driver Controlled Period{{/BOLD}} - A time period during which {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} operate their {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} via remote control.
{{BOLD}}Match Load{{/BOLD}} - One of the 24 Blocks, 12 per Alliance, that begin the Match in an Alliance Station and which may be introduced during the Match. See <SG9> for more information.
{{BOLD}}Parked{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} status at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. See <SC4>.

{{BOLD}}Qualification Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} used to determine Team rankings for {{LINK:#allianceselection}}Alliance Selection{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}} consists of two {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} competing to earn {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Robot{{/BOLD}} - A machine that has passed inspection, designed by {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} Team members to execute one or more tasks autonomously and/or by remote control from a {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Member{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Robot Skills Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#drivingskillsmatch}}Driving Skills Match{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#autonomouscodingskillsmatch}}Autonomous Coding Skills Match{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}Scored{{/BOLD}} - A Block status. See <SC2>.
{{BOLD}}Sensor{{/BOLD}} - A device that detects and responds to changes in the environment, providing data to the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot’s{{/LINK}} control system.

{{BOLD}}Skills Stop Time{{/BOLD}} - The time remaining in a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} when a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} ends the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early.
  
a. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} does not end the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} early, they receive a default {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} of 0.
b. The moment when the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} ends early is defined as the moment when the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is “{{LINK:#disablement}}Disabled{{/LINK}}” by the field control system. See the “Skills Stop Time” section for more details.
c. If a V5 Robot Brain or Tournament Manager display is being used for field control, then the {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} is the time shown on the display when the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is ended early (i.e., in 1-second increments).
d. If a VEXnet Competition Switch is being used for field control, in conjunction with a manual timer that counts down to 0 with greater accuracy than 1-second increments, then the time shown on the timer should be rounded up to the nearest second. For example, if the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#disablement}}Disabled{{/LINK}} and the timer shows 25.2 seconds, then the {{LINK:#skillsstoptime}}Skills Stop Time{{/LINK}} should be recorded as 26.

{{BOLD}}Strength of Schedule Points (SP){{/BOLD}} - The third basis of ranking {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}} are equivalent to the score of the losing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} in a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}. In the event of a tie, both {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} receive {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}} equal to the tie score. If both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} on an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} are {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}}, the {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} on the not {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} will receive their own score as {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}} for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Student{{/BOLD}} - A person is considered a {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} if they meet both of the following criteria:
 
 
a. Anyone who is earning or has earned credit toward a secondary school (i.e., high school) diploma, certificate, or other equivalent during the six (6) months preceding the VEX Robotics World Championship. Courses earning credits leading up to high school would satisfy this requirement.
b. Anyone born after May 1, 2006 (i.e., who will be 19 or younger at VEX Worlds 2026). Eligibility may also be granted based on a disability that has delayed education by at least one year.

  
  • {{BOLD}}Middle School Student{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} born after May 1, 2010 (i.e., who will be 15 or younger at VEX Worlds 2026). Any {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} who meet this criteria may also compete as {{LINK:#student}}High School Students{{/LINK}}.
  • {{BOLD}}High School Student{{/BOLD}} - Any eligible {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} that is not a {{LINK:#student}}Middle School Student{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Team{{/BOLD}} - One or more {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} make up a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. To participate in an official VEX V5 Robotics Competition event, a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} must first register on RobotEvents.com and receive a VEX V5 Robotics Competition {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} number. A {{LINK:#team}}Team's{{/LINK}} unique number identifies their organization and their {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} within that organization. Each {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} must build their own {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}, create their own code, and maintain their own Engineering Notebook if they choose to use one.
  
• A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is classified as a Middle School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} if all members are {{LINK:#middleschoolstudent}}Middle School Students{{/LINK}}.
• A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is classified as a High School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} if any of its members are {{LINK:#highschoolstudent}}High School Students{{/LINK}}, or if the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} is made up of {{LINK:#middleschoolstudent}}Middle School Students{{/LINK}} who declare themselves “playing up” as {{LINK:#highschoolstudent}}High School Students{{/LINK}} by registering their {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} as a High School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}.
• Once a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} has competed in an event as a High School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}, that {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} may not change back to a Middle School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} for the remainder of the season. If a {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} mistakenly registers as a Middle School {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} but is ineligible for that age group, their registration may be revised mid-season with RSM assistance; all prior qualifications for the season will be lost.
• {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} may be associated with schools, community/youth organizations, or groups of neighborhood {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}}. In the context of this Game Manual, {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} include three types of {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}} roles related to {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} build, design, and coding. See <G2> and <G5> for more information. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} may not fulfill any of these roles.
 
 
• {{BOLD}}Builder{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}(s) on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who assemble(s) and repair(s) the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are permitted to teach the {{LINK:#team}}Builder{{/LINK}}(s) how to use concepts or tools associated with {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} construction, but should never work on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Coder{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}(s) on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who write(s) the computer code that is downloaded onto the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are permitted to teach the {{LINK:#team}}Coder{{/LINK}}(s) how to use concepts or tools associated with programming, but should never work on the code that goes on the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Designer{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#student}}Student{{/LINK}}(s) on the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who design(s) the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#adult}}Adults{{/LINK}} are permitted to teach the {{LINK:#team}}Designer{{/LINK}}(s) how to use concepts or tools associated with design, but should never work on the design of the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Violation{{/BOLD}} - The act of breaking a rule in the Game Manual.
  
• {{BOLD}}Minor Violation{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} which does not result in a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}.

  
  • Accidental, momentary, or otherwise non {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} are usually {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}}.
  • {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} usually result in a verbal notification from the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, which should serve to inform the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that a rule is being Violated before it escalates to a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Major Violation{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} which results in a {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualification{{/LINK}}.

  
  • Unless otherwise noted in a rule, all {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} are {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}}.
  • If noted in the rule, egregious or strategic {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} or intentional actions that result in {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} may also be {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}}.
  • Multiple {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} within a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} or tournament may escalate to a {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} at the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee’s{{/LINK}} discretion or as specified in a rule. {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violations{{/LINK}} carry over into Eliminations unless otherwise specified within a rule.
• {{BOLD}}Match Affecting{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} which changes the winning and losing {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} in the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

  
  • Multiple {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} within a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} can cumulatively become {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}.
  • When evaluating if a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} was {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} will focus primarily on any {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} actions that were directly related to the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}.
  • Determining whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} was {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} can only be done once the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} is complete and the scores have been calculated.
• {{BOLD}}CoC-related Violation{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} of a rule that relates to the RECF Code of Conduct and related processes. For V5RC, VURC, and VAIRC, the included rules are: <S2>, {{LINK:#winpoints}}<G1>{{/LINK}},<G2>, <G4>, <G5>, <R1> through <R4>, and {{LINK:#violation}}Major Violations{{/LINK}} of <S1>.

  
  • Potential {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} should be brought to the attention of the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner{{/LINK}}, and resolved through the Code of Conduct Reporting Process in coordination with the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}, Judge Advisor, and RECF Regional Support Manager. Follow-up investigations are conducted by the RECF Rules & Conduct Committee (RCC). Penalties for confirmed {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} can range from single-{{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualifications{{/LINK}} to removal from the program. Event staff other than referees may review video footage and/or photographs from the event as part of {{LINK:#violation}}CoC-related Violations{{/LINK}} investigations; these reviews cannot change gameplay rulings made by {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}}. Some rules include Violation Notes in red italicized text to denote special circumstances or provide additional clarifications. If no Violation Notes are found in a given rule, then it should be assumed that the above “default” definitions apply.
 To determine whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} may have been {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}, check whether the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} who committed the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} won or lost the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. If they did not win the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, then the {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} could not have been {{LINK:#violation}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}, and it was very likely a {{LINK:#violation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}}.
 See the flowcharts in figures V-1, V-2, and V-3 for more information, and {{LINK:https://kb.roboticseducation.org/hc/en-us/articles/14490397054359-Code-of-Conduct-Reporting-Process}}this article{{/LINK}} for full details of the event CoC process.

{{BOLD}}Win Points (WP){{/BOLD}} - The first basis of ranking {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} will receive zero (0), one (1), two (2), or three (3) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}} for each {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}. Unless a Team is {{LINK:#disqualification}}Disqualified{{/LINK}}, both {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} on an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} always earn the same number of {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}.
  
• One (1) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Point{{/LINK}} is awarded for completing the {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} task(s).
• Two (2) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}} are awarded for winning a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}.
• One (1) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Point{{/LINK}} is awarded for tying a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}.
• Zero (0) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}} are awarded for losing a {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}.

gameSpecificDefinitions.definition:
{{BOLD}}<RSC1>{{/BOLD}} {{BOLD}}Standard rules apply in most cases.{{/BOLD}} All rules from previous sections apply to {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}, unless otherwise specified in this section.
  
a. Removing {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from the Field in a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} is not a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} that leave the Field cannot be returned.
{{VIOLATION_NOTES}}Violation Note:{{/VIOLATION_NOTES}}
  
• In the Robot Skills Challenge, the standard definition of {{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}} does not apply, because there is no winner or loser. When evaluating whether a rule {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} should be classified as a {{LINK:#majorviolation}}Major Violation{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#minorviolation}}Minor Violation{{/LINK}} in the context of this criteria, the term “score affecting” can be substituted for “{{LINK:#matchaffecting}}Match Affecting{{/LINK}}.” A {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} is considered “score affecting” if it results in a net increase of that {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} score at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
• {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} of <GG>, <SG>, and <RSC> rules that occur during a {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Match{{/LINK}} should only affect the outcome of that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and should not be considered when determining whether a {{LINK:#violation}}Violation{{/LINK}} has been repeated during the event.

{{BOLD}}Additional Electronics{{/BOLD}} - Any {{LINK:#sensor}}Sensor{{/LINK}}, processor, or other electronic component used in {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} construction, and connected to the V5 Robot Brain, that is not sold by VEX Robotics. Examples include commercially-available devices (e.g., Raspberry Pi) or custom devices designed and fabricated by the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}}. See <VUR12> and <VUR13> for more details.
{{BOLD}}Autonomous Line{{/BOLD}} - The pair of white tape lines that run across the Field, and the space between those lines. See <SG7> for more information.

{{BOLD}}Block{{/BOLD}} - A blue or red 18-sided hollow plastic polygonal object with flat faces and a weight of approximately 40 grams. Each cross-section measures approximately 3.25” (82mm) between pairs of opposing flat faces, and 3.85” (98mm) between pairs of opposing corners.

{{BOLD}}Bullseye{{/BOLD}} – The square at the center of the printed {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} images on the Push Back graphic field tiles. (See Figure BE-1.) Only used in VAIRC {{LINK:#robotskillsmatch}}Robot Skills Matches{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Control Bonus{{/BOLD}} – One of four bonuses that can be earned by having a {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} of your {{LINK:#team}}Team’s{{/LINK}} color {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}} as the outermost {{LINK:#block}}Block{{/LINK}} in one end of a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} and the nearest corresponding end of a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}} (see figure CB-1).

{{BOLD}}Control Zone{{/BOLD}} - A defined section of a {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} that can be {{LINK:#controlled}}Controlled{{/LINK}} by an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} at the end of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.
  
• {{BOLD}}Long Goal{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} for a {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} consists of the space between (but not including) the white tape lines (highlighted in green in Figure CZ-1), and holds up to three (3) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Center Goal, Upper and Lower{{/BOLD}} - The {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} for a {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}} includes the entire {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Controlled{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} status that is assessed at the end of the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} and the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. A {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} is {{LINK:#controlled}}Controlled{{/LINK}} by an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} if a majority of the {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} in that {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}} are that {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance’s{{/LINK}} color. See rule <SC3> for details.

{{BOLD}}Fabricated Part{{/BOLD}} - Any component used in {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} construction that is fabricated by {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} members. See <VUR3>, through <VUR7> for more details.

{{BOLD}}Game Design Committee (GDC){{/BOLD}} - The creators of Push Back, and authors of this Game Manual. The GDC is the only official source for rules clarifications and Q&A responses; see Section 1.

{{BOLD}}Goal{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} that is constructed out of plastic and metal components into which {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} can be {{LINK:#scored}}Scored{{/LINK}}. Each {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} has a completely enclosed center section between two open sections. Each {{LINK:#goal}}Goal{{/LINK}} includes a defined {{LINK:#controlzone}}Control Zone{{/LINK}}.
  
• {{BOLD}}Long Goal{{/BOLD}} - Each {{LINK:#goal}}Long Goal{{/LINK}} is 48.8” (1239mm) in length, with a 13.33” (339mm) enclosed center section.
• {{BOLD}}Center Goal, Upper and Lower{{/BOLD}} – Each {{LINK:#goal}}Center Goal{{/LINK}} is 22.6” (574mm) in length.

{{BOLD}}Interaction Period{{/BOLD}} – The one-minute 45-second (1:45) time period that follows the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} after the winner of the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} has been determined. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} react only to sensor inputs and to commands pre-programmed by the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} into the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} control system and can interact with the entire {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#interactionperiod}}Interaction Period{{/LINK}} replaces the {{LINK:#drivercontrolledperiod}}Driver Controlled Period{{/LINK}} that occurs in V5RC and VURC {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Isolation Bonus{{/BOLD}} – A point bonus awarded to the {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} that has earned the most points at the end of the {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#isolationbonus}}Isolation Bonus{{/LINK}} replaces the {{LINK:#autonomousbonus}}Autonomous Bonus{{/LINK}}. See rule <VAISC3>.

{{BOLD}}Isolation Period{{/BOLD}} – A 15-second (0:15) time period during which {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} operate only on their side of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} and react only to sensor inputs and to commands pre-programmed by the {{LINK:#student}}Students{{/LINK}} into the {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} control system. This {{LINK:#isolationperiod}}Isolation Period{{/LINK}} replaces the {{LINK:#autonomousperiod}}Autonomous Period{{/LINK}} normally found in a VURC {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Isolation Win Point{{/BOLD}} – One (1) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Point{{/LINK}} (WP) given to an {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliance{{/LINK}} that has completed the {{LINK:#isolationwinpoint}}Isolation Win Point{{/LINK}} criteria set forth in rule <VAISC4>. The {{LINK:#isolationwinpoint}}Isolation Win Point{{/LINK}} replaces the {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Holding{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} status; see rule <GG17> for more information. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is considered to be {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} if it meets any of the following criteria during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}:
 
• {{BOLD}}Trapping{{/BOLD}} - Limiting the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to a small or confined area of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, approximately the size of one foam field tile or less, without an avenue for escape. Note that if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not attempting to escape, it is not considered {{LINK:#trap}}Trapped{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Pinning{{/BOLD}} - Preventing the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} through contact with the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}, or another {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Lifting{{/BOLD}} - Controlling an opponent’s movements by raising or tilting the opponent’s {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} off of the foam tiles. Preventing a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that is already off of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} from returning to the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} may also be considered {{LINK:#lift}}Lifting{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#trap}}Trapping{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Loader{{/BOLD}} - One of four 21.34” (542mm) tall plastic and rubber structures that are attached to the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} may remove {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} from {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#driveteammember}}Drive Team Members{{/LINK}} may add {{LINK:#matchload}}Match Load{{/LINK}} {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} to {{LINK:#loader}}Loaders{{/LINK}} during the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} (see <SG9> for details). Each {{LINK:#loader}}Loader{{/LINK}} begins the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} containing (6) {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Match Schedule{{/BOLD}} - A list of {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}} that is generated at the start of an event. The {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} includes the predetermined, randomly-paired {{LINK:#alliance}}Alliances{{/LINK}} that will be competing in each {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Match{{/LINK}}, and the expected start times for these {{LINK:#match}}Matches{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#matchschedule}}Match Schedule{{/LINK}} may be subject to change at the {{LINK:#eventpartner}}Event Partner’s{{/LINK}} discretion.

{{BOLD}}Neutral Zone{{/BOLD}} - An area of the Field defined by and including white tape lines, which encompasses the {{LINK:#centergoal}}Center Goals{{/LINK}} and their surrounding space. See VURS-1.

{{BOLD}}Park Zone{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}} and enclosed section of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} that mark a location where {{LINK:#block}}Blocks{{/LINK}} begin a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} and {{LINK:#robot}}Robots{{/LINK}} can be {{LINK:#parked}}Parked{{/LINK}} at the end of the {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zones{{/LINK}} are made of red or blue plastic extrusions and black plastic connectors, and include the enclosed portions of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}} Tiles. Each {{LINK:#parkzone}}Park Zone{{/LINK}} is 18.87“ (479mm) wide x 16.86” (428mm) deep.

{{BOLD}}Holding{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} status; see rule <GG17> for more information. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is considered to be {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} if it meets any of the following criteria during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}:
 
• {{BOLD}}Trapping{{/BOLD}} - Limiting the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to a small or confined area of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, approximately the size of one foam field tile or less, without an avenue for escape. Note that if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not attempting to escape, it is not considered {{LINK:#trap}}Trapped{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Pinning{{/BOLD}} - Preventing the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} through contact with the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}, or another {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Lifting{{/BOLD}} - Controlling an opponent’s movements by raising or tilting the opponent’s {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} off of the foam tiles. Preventing a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that is already off of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} from returning to the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} may also be considered {{LINK:#lift}}Lifting{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#trap}}Trapping{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Practice Match{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}} used to provide time for {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} and volunteers to get acquainted with the official playing Field and procedures. {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}} earn {{LINK:#team}}Teams{{/LINK}} zero (0) {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}}, {{LINK:#autonomouspoints}}Autonomous Points{{/LINK}}, and {{LINK:#strengthofschedulepoints}}Strength of Schedule Points{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referees{{/LINK}} should not record or track standard gameplay {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} that occur during {{LINK:#practicematch}}Practice Matches{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#violation}}Violations{{/LINK}} that are egregious, unsportsmanlike and/or unsafe may be recorded and tracked at the discretion of the {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}.

 Head Referees have ultimate and final authority on all gameplay and Robot ruling decisions during the competition.
{{BOLD}}Raw Stock{{/BOLD}} - Stock materials purchased from third-party vendors that may be used to create {{LINK:#fabricatedparts}}Fabricated Parts{{/LINK}}. See <VUR4> and <VUR5>.

{{BOLD}}Scorekeeper Referee{{/BOLD}} - An impartial volunteer responsible for tallying scores at the end of a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. {{LINK:#scorekeeperreferee}}Scorekeeper Referees{{/LINK}} do not make ruling interpretations, and should redirect any Team questions regarding rules or scores to a {{LINK:#headreferee}}Head Referee{{/LINK}}.

{{BOLD}}Holding{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} status; see rule <GG17> for more information. A {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is considered to be {{LINK:#hold}}Holding{{/LINK}} if it meets any of the following criteria during a {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}:
 
• {{BOLD}}Trapping{{/BOLD}} - Limiting the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} to a small or confined area of the {{LINK:#field}}Field{{/LINK}}, approximately the size of one foam field tile or less, without an avenue for escape. Note that if a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} is not attempting to escape, it is not considered {{LINK:#trap}}Trapped{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Pinning{{/BOLD}} - Preventing the movement of an opponent {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} through contact with the {{LINK:#fieldperimeter}}Field Perimeter{{/LINK}}, a {{LINK:#fieldelement}}Field Element{{/LINK}}, or another {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}}.
• {{BOLD}}Lifting{{/BOLD}} - Controlling an opponent’s movements by raising or tilting the opponent’s {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} off of the foam tiles. Preventing a {{LINK:#robot}}Robot{{/LINK}} that is already off of the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} from returning to the {{LINK:#floor}}Floor{{/LINK}} may also be considered {{LINK:#lift}}Lifting{{/LINK}} or {{LINK:#trap}}Trapping{{/LINK}}.
{{BOLD}}VEX U Student{{/BOLD}} - A {{LINK:#team}}Team{{/LINK}} member that meets all criteria listed in rule <VUT6>.

{{BOLD}}Win Percentage (WP){{/BOLD}} - Replaces {{LINK:#winpoints}}Win Points{{/LINK}} in a league event. {{LINK:#winpercentage}}Win Percentage{{/LINK}} is calculated by the number of wins divided by the number of {{LINK:#qualificationmatch}}Qualification Matches{{/LINK}} the Team plays. In cases of a tie, the Team is given a 0.5 number of “wins” for that {{LINK:#match}}Match{{/LINK}}. The {{LINK:#autonomouswinpoint}}Autonomous Win Point{{/LINK}} is also considered 0.5 “wins,” added to the total number of wins.
